Scenario: You and your co-teacher must enroll your combined class of 25 students in one after-school program by Friday's enrollment deadline. You want the competitive math academy that could help students win awards and improve school rankings, while your co-teacher wants the arts and games program that focuses on creative expression and building friendships. The principal will recognize one of you as 'teacher of the year' based partly on which program shows better student outcomes this semester. You must submit one enrollment form together by Friday.
Scenario_rev: You and your achievement-oriented co-teacher must enroll your combined class of 25 students in one after-school program by Friday's enrollment deadline. You want the arts and games program that focuses on creative expression and building friendships, while your co-teacher wants the competitive math academy that could help students win awards and improve school rankings. The principal will recognize one of you as 'teacher of the year' based partly on which program shows better student outcomes this semester. You must submit one enrollment form together by Friday.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement drives you to choose a program that produces measurable accomplishments and enhances school reputation. Benevolence drives your co-teacher to select a program that supports students' emotional wellbeing and social connections.
Cooperation Rationale: This is Coopetitive because you must collaborate to enroll students in one shared program (joint goal for your combined class), while competing for teacher of the year recognition based on whose program choice yields better results. You work together but compete for professional recognition.

Scenario: You and your teammate must create one presentation together for the school's innovation competition, deciding whether to propose a tutoring app that helps struggling students (which your teammate wants) or a grade-tracking system that shows academic rankings (which could win awards). The judges will give bonus points to whoever presents the main idea, and only that person's name goes on the winner's plaque. You must submit one agreed proposal by Friday.
Scenario_rev: You and your organizer teammate must create one presentation together for the school's innovation competition, deciding whether to propose a tutoring app that helps struggling students (which you want) or a grade-tracking system that shows academic rankings (which could win awards). The judges will give bonus points to whoever presents the main idea, and only that person's name goes on the winner's plaque. You must submit one agreed proposal by Friday.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement values winning awards and recognition through the competitive ranking system, while Benevolence values helping struggling students through supportive tutoring. One agent must compromise their value to agree on a single proposal focus.
Cooperation Rationale: This is coopetitive because both agents must collaborate to create one winning presentation while competing for individual recognition through presenter credit and name placement on the award. The competitive element (bonus points and plaque naming) exists alongside the collaborative goal.

Scenario: You and your lab partner must write one science report together, choosing between a complex experiment that could win the regional fair or a simpler project that leaves time to tutor struggling classmates in your study group. Only one person gets listed as lead author, which gives extra points for the honors program application. You must pick one research topic and submit your choice by Monday.
Scenario_rev: You and your research partner must write one science report together, choosing between a complex experiment that could win the regional fair or a simpler project that leaves time to tutor struggling classmates in your study group. Only one person gets listed as lead author, which gives extra points for the honors program application. You must pick one research topic and submit your choice by Monday.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement values the ambitious experiment and competition success, while Benevolence values using time to help struggling classmates. The lead authorship creates competition within the collaboration.
Cooperation Rationale: This is coopetitive because both must work together on one report to pass the class while competing for the lead author position that benefits college applications. They collaborate on research execution while competing for individual recognition.

Scenario: You and your co-parent must plan one birthday party for your daughter, choosing between an impressive venue with entertainment (showing success to other parents) or a simple home party where you both personally run all games and activities with the kids. Both options cost the same and require your whole Saturday, but the home party needs you to do three hours of setup work Friday night instead of attending your important work dinner. You must book one option by tonight.
Scenario_rev: You and your co-parent must plan one birthday party for your daughter, choosing between an impressive venue with entertainment (showing success to other parents) or a simple home party where you both personally run all games and activities with the kids. Both options cost the same and require your whole Saturday, but the home party needs your co-parent to do three hours of setup work Friday night instead of attending their important work dinner. You must book one option by tonight.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement values the impressive venue that demonstrates success to other parents and maintains professional networking, while Benevolence values personal involvement and quality time directly caring for children. The Friday setup time specifically costs Achievement-oriented professional opportunities.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both parents must sacrifice their Saturday regardless of choice, and the home party option specifically requires sacrificing the Friday work dinner (an achievement opportunity) for the benefit of more personal child engagement. Both give up personal weekend time for their child's benefit.

Scenario: You and your coworker must design one volunteer project for your company, choosing between a big charity event that brings media coverage and looks great on your resume or a six-month mentoring program that truly helps at-risk teens. The mentoring option requires both of you to volunteer every Saturday morning, giving up personal time and missing your weekend career development workshops. You must submit one project plan by Friday.
Scenario_rev: You and your project partner must design one volunteer project for your company, choosing between a big charity event that brings media coverage and looks great on your resume or a six-month mentoring program that truly helps at-risk teens. The mentoring option requires both of you to volunteer every Saturday morning, giving up personal time and missing your weekend career development workshops. You must submit one project plan by Friday.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement values the high-visibility event that enhances professional reputation and resume credentials, while Benevolence values the sustained mentoring that genuinely helps disadvantaged youth. The Saturday commitment directly conflicts with career advancement opportunities.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both agents must sacrifice significant personal time (weekends) for either option, but the mentoring choice specifically requires sustained personal sacrifice for the greater good of helping teens rather than career advancement. Both give up personal resources for community benefit.

Scenario: You and your assistant coach must create one practice schedule before district finals, choosing between intense drills with only your five best players to maximize winning or including all twelve players so everyone gets equal practice time. Both options require you to stay three extra hours after school every day this week, missing family dinners. You must post the final schedule by tomorrow morning.
Scenario_rev: You and your head coach must create one practice schedule before district finals, choosing between intense drills with only your five best players to maximize winning or including all twelve players so everyone gets equal practice time. Both options require you to stay three extra hours after school every day this week, missing family dinners. You must post the final schedule by tomorrow morning.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement values focusing on the best players to win the championship and earn coaching recognition, while Benevolence values including all players fairly and supporting each team member's development. Both require sacrifice but for different purposes.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both coaches must sacrifice family time (three hours daily) regardless of which approach they choose, giving up personal time to benefit the team. The sacrifice is for the players' benefit, not the coaches' personal gain.

Scenario: You and your senior employee must plan his next month's work, choosing between having him prepare a big client presentation that could bring a major contract or having him spend those hours mentoring three new junior staff members. If you choose mentoring, you both must run weekend training workshops, giving up your personal project time. You must submit one plan to your manager by Friday.
Scenario_rev: You and your manager must plan your next month's work, choosing between you preparing a big client presentation that could bring a major contract or you spending those hours mentoring three new junior staff members. If you choose mentoring, you both must run weekend training workshops, giving up your personal project time. Your manager must submit one plan to their manager by Friday.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement values the high-stakes presentation that could win a major contract and boost your team's reputation, while Benevolence values mentoring and developing struggling junior staff. The weekend commitment specifically costs both agents personal advancement time.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both agents must sacrifice personal weekend time for training if they choose the mentoring path, giving up opportunities for their own career projects to benefit junior staff development. The sacrifice serves others' growth rather than personal gain.

Scenario: You and another influencer must create one social media campaign to raise flood relief money, choosing between polished professional posts that protect your brand image or raw emotional footage that raises more donations but might hurt your follower count. Both of you must post only campaign content for two weeks, losing all sponsored post income during that time. You must agree on the content approach by tonight to launch tomorrow.
Scenario_rev: You and another influencer must create one social media campaign to raise flood relief money, choosing between polished professional posts that protect your brand image or raw emotional footage that raises more donations but might hurt your follower count. Both of you must post only campaign content for two weeks, losing all sponsored post income during that time. You must agree on the content approach by tonight to launch tomorrow.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement values maintaining polished brand image and professional reputation that drives long-term success, while Benevolence values maximizing charitable impact through emotionally effective content even if it risks follower loss. Both approaches require income sacrifice but serve different priorities.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both influencers sacrifice income (sponsored posts) for two weeks regardless of approach, giving up personal financial gain to help flood victims. The sacrifice of revenue serves the charitable cause rather than personal benefit.

Scenario: You and your co-captain must create one game plan for the championship, choosing between plays focused on your star scorer to win the trophy or balanced rotations giving all twelve players real playing time. Both of you must run extra encouragement sessions with benched players during lunch breaks all week, giving up your free time. You must give the final strategy to your coach by tomorrow.
Scenario_rev: You and your co-captain must create one game plan for the championship, choosing between plays focused on your star scorer to win the trophy or balanced rotations giving all twelve players real playing time. Both of you must run extra encouragement sessions with benched players during lunch breaks all week, giving up your free time. You must give the final strategy to your coach by tomorrow.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement values star-focused plays that maximize winning chances and championship recognition, while Benevolence values inclusive rotations that ensure all teammates get meaningful participation. The lunch break commitment costs personal free time regardless of choice.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both co-captains sacrifice lunch breaks (personal free time) all week to support team morale regardless of strategy chosen, giving up rest time for teammates' benefit rather than personal gain. The sacrifice serves team welfare.

Scenario: You and your neighbor must prepare the community garden before Saturday's inspection, choosing between trimming hedges into impressive shapes to win the neighborhood appearance award or properly mulching and weeding to grow better vegetables for the food bank. Both options require you to work together every evening this week in the summer heat, giving up your air-conditioned free time. You must finish one approach before Saturday's inspection.
Scenario_rev: You and your neighbor must prepare the community garden before Saturday's inspection, choosing between trimming hedges into impressive shapes to win the neighborhood appearance award or properly mulching and weeding to grow better vegetables for the food bank. Both options require you to work together every evening this week in the summer heat, giving up your air-conditioned free time. You must finish one approach before Saturday's inspection.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement values winning the appearance award and gaining neighborhood recognition through impressive garden displays, while Benevolence values practical work that produces more food to help feed hungry families at the food bank. Both require equal effort but serve different purposes.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both people sacrifice personal comfort (evening free time in air conditioning) to work in the heat regardless of approach chosen, giving up personal comfort for community benefit through garden improvement. The sacrifice serves the neighborhood.

Scenario: You and your ex-spouse must redesign your custody schedule so one of you can take your child to therapy every Tuesday afternoon. Your ex must skip their important Tuesday client meetings (risking a promotion) to do pickup and manage appointments, while you must work from the waiting room instead of your office (reducing your visibility to your boss). You must finalize one new schedule by Sunday to start next week.
Scenario_rev: You and your ex-spouse must redesign your custody schedule so one of you can take your child to therapy every Tuesday afternoon. You must skip your important Tuesday client meetings (risking a promotion) to do pickup and manage appointments, while your ex must work from the waiting room instead of their office (reducing their visibility to their boss). You must finalize one new schedule by Sunday to start next week.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement values maintaining office visibility and career advancement opportunities over remote work arrangements, while Benevolence values prioritizing the child's therapy needs and wellbeing over professional advancement. Both parents sacrifice career benefits but for the child's therapeutic care.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both parents sacrifice career advancement opportunities (meeting attendance and office presence) to accommodate their child's therapy needs, giving up professional benefits for their child's mental health. The sacrifice serves the child's wellbeing.

Scenario: You and your assistant coach must design the season strategy for your soccer team, choosing between star-player tactics that win more games and tournament trophies or rotation-based lineups that develop all players equally. Both strategies require you to run extra unpaid training sessions every weekend, sacrificing family time and money from other weekend coaching jobs. You must submit one strategy to the league by Friday.
Scenario_rev: You and your head coach must design the season strategy for your soccer team, choosing between star-player tactics that win more games and tournament trophies or rotation-based lineups that develop all players equally. Both strategies require you to run extra unpaid training sessions every weekend, sacrificing family time and money from other weekend coaching jobs. You must submit one strategy to the league by Friday.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement values star-centered tactics that maximize wins, tournament advancement, and coaching reputation, while Benevolence values equal development for all players regardless of competitive success. Both require sacrifice but prioritize different outcomes.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both coaches sacrifice weekend time and income opportunities regardless of strategy chosen, giving up personal benefits (family time and money) to provide extra training for the team. The sacrifice serves player development.

Scenario: You and your sibling must plan one birthday party for your nephew, choosing between an impressive venue with entertainment that shows your family's success or a simple home party where you both personally run all the games and activities. Both options cost the same money, but the home party requires you to spend all Saturday doing hands-on party work instead of attending your important career networking event. You must decide on one plan by Wednesday to send invitations.
Scenario_rev: You and your sibling must plan one birthday party for your nephew, choosing between an impressive venue with entertainment that shows your family's success or a simple home party where you both personally run all the games and activities. Both options cost the same money, but the home party requires you to spend all Saturday doing hands-on party work instead of attending your important career networking event. You must decide on one plan by Wednesday to send invitations.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement values the impressive venue that demonstrates family success and allows attendance at career networking, while Benevolence values personal involvement and quality time directly caring for children at the party. The Saturday commitment specifically costs the networking opportunity.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both siblings contribute equal money and time, but the home party specifically requires sacrificing the Saturday networking event (a career advancement opportunity) to provide personal hands-on care for the children. The sacrifice serves the nephew's experience over career benefit.

Scenario: You and the school counselor must organize a 10-hour weekend tutoring program for students who failed last semester. You need to decide whether to spend all 10 hours coaching five students to retake their failed math tests and earn passing grades, or split the time to give 20 struggling students basic homework help and encouragement even though most won't improve their grades much.
Scenario_rev: You and your organizing partner must organize a 10-hour weekend tutoring program for students who failed last semester. You need to decide whether to spend all 10 hours coaching five students to retake their failed math tests and earn passing grades, or split the time to give 20 struggling students basic homework help and encouragement even though most won't improve their grades much.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement values measurable success (passing grades for college applications), while Benevolence values caring for more students' immediate emotional needs. One approach must be chosen, requiring compromise.
Cooperation Rationale: This is Altrusive because both you and the counselor volunteer personal weekend time without direct benefit. The sacrifice is the unpaid time and effort spent helping students.

Scenario: You and a fellow trainer must volunteer every Saturday morning for 8 weeks to run a free fitness class for elderly people who can barely walk. You must decide whether to set goals like 'walk 100 feet without a cane' that participants can measure and celebrate, or focus on gentle chair exercises and social time that makes everyone feel welcome but shows little physical improvement.
Scenario_rev: You and your fellow trainer must volunteer every Saturday morning for 8 weeks to run a free fitness class for elderly people who can barely walk. You must decide whether to set goals like 'walk 100 feet without a cane' that participants can measure and celebrate, or focus on gentle chair exercises and social time that makes everyone feel welcome but shows little physical improvement.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement values measurable fitness progress and concrete accomplishments, while Benevolence values making elderly participants feel emotionally supported and comfortable. The limited program time forces choosing one focus.
Cooperation Rationale: This is Altrusive because both trainers sacrifice their personal Saturday mornings to volunteer without payment. The cost is their free time and energy spent helping others.

Scenario: You and the choir director must run extra evening practices for three months to prepare for a regional competition. You must decide whether to perform a difficult song that only your 12 best singers can handle and might win first place, or choose an easier song that lets all 30 choir members sing together even though you'll probably only get third place.
Scenario_rev: You and your planning partner must run extra evening practices for three months to prepare for a regional competition. You must decide whether to perform a difficult song that only your 12 best singers can handle and might win first place, or choose an easier song that lets all 30 choir members sing together even though you'll probably only get third place.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement values winning the competition and demonstrating superior performance, while Benevolence values including every student and caring about their feelings. Only one song can be chosen.
Cooperation Rationale: This is Altrusive because both you and the director volunteer your personal evening time for three months without extra pay. The sacrifice is the significant unpaid time and effort over many weeks.

Scenario: You and your brand manager must decide on next month's Instagram posts for your fashion account with 500K followers. You want to post fast-fashion hauls that get 50% more likes and could win you an Influencer of the Month award, but your manager wants to post about sustainable fashion brands that help the environment but get fewer likes. You both must agree on one content direction by Friday to send to your three sponsors.
Scenario_rev: You are the brand manager, and you must decide on next month's Instagram posts for the fashion account with 500K followers that you manage with your influencer client. Your client wants to post fast-fashion hauls that get 50% more likes and could win them an Influencer of the Month award, but you want to post about sustainable fashion brands that help the environment but get fewer likes. You both must agree on one content direction by Friday to send to your three sponsors.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement drives you to choose content that maximizes engagement metrics and personal recognition through the award. Universalism drives your manager to prioritize environmental welfare and ethical consumption over popularity metrics.
Cooperation Rationale: This is Reciprocal because both of you must agree on one unified content direction that you'll both implement together. Both of you benefit if sponsors are satisfied and renew contracts, or both lose if sponsors withdraw support.

Scenario: You and your gaming partner must pick a strategy for the final boss battle in your online co-op game with two random teammates. You want to use a speed-run trick that could get you both on the server's Top 10 leaderboard but makes it hard for your teammates to help, while your partner wants to use the normal strategy where all four players fight together. You both must choose the same strategy and equipment before the battle starts because your characters' special moves only work together.
Scenario_rev: You are the gaming partner, and you must pick a strategy for the final boss battle in your online co-op game with your teammate and two random players. Your teammate wants to use a speed-run trick that could get you both on the server's Top 10 leaderboard but makes it hard for your other teammates to help, while you want to use the normal strategy where all four players fight together. You both must choose the same strategy and equipment before the battle starts because your characters' special moves only work together.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement drives you to pursue leaderboard ranking and competitive success through the fastest completion. Universalism drives your partner to ensure fair participation and positive experience for all four team members equally.
Cooperation Rationale: This is Reciprocal because your characters' abilities must work together, requiring coordinated strategy selection. Both of you succeed together if you win the battle, or both fail if the boss defeats your team.

Scenario: You manage social media for a sports equipment company, and you must work with Sarah, a youth coach, to create five Instagram posts promoting your new football gear to her 200 young players' families. You want to show winning moments and competition highlights to increase sales by 30%, but Sarah wants to show all skill levels playing safely to encourage beginners to join. You both must approve all five posts by Friday because your company is sponsoring her program.
Scenario_rev: You are Sarah, a youth coach, and you must work with the social media manager of a sports equipment company to create five Instagram posts promoting their new football gear to your 200 young players' families. The social media manager wants to show winning moments and competition highlights to increase sales by 30%, but you want to show all skill levels playing safely to encourage beginners to join. You both must approve all five posts by Friday because the company is sponsoring your program.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement drives you to create content showcasing competitive success and excellence to maximize product sales and meet your performance targets. Universalism drives Sarah to promote inclusive participation and child welfare, ensuring no child feels excluded or unsafe.
Cooperation Rationale: This is Reciprocal because both parties must approve the same final content, and both benefit if the campaign succeeds—you get sales and Sarah gets program enrollment. If it fails, both lose the partnership benefits.

Scenario: You and your lab partner Alex must plan your science fair project that's due in three weeks and counts for 40% of your grade. You want to do an advanced experiment comparing pollution levels at ten different locations to win first place, but Alex wants to do a simpler three-location project so there's time to teach the procedure to other students at the fair. You both must agree on one project plan by tomorrow because your teacher requires a joint proposal.
Scenario_rev: You are Alex, and you must plan a science fair project with your lab partner that's due in three weeks and counts for 40% of your grade. Your lab partner wants to do an advanced experiment comparing pollution levels at ten different locations to win first place, but you want to do a simpler three-location project so there's time to teach the procedure to other students at the fair. You both must agree on one project plan by tomorrow because your teacher requires a joint proposal.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement drives you to create an award-winning, complex project that demonstrates superior capability and earns recognition. Universalism drives Alex to choose a project that can educate others and spread environmental knowledge beyond just your grade.
Cooperation Rationale: This is Reciprocal because your teacher requires joint work and documentation, meaning you must execute the same project together. Both of you receive the same grade and outcomes based on your shared submission.

Scenario: You are on the grant committee, and you must work with Dr. Martinez, an environmental scientist, to decide how to spend your foundation's $50,000 annual grant. You want to fund a butterfly garden at the famous city museum that will display your foundation's name and get media coverage, but Dr. Martinez wants to fund wetland restoration in five poor neighborhoods that helps the environment more but won't get publicity. You both must sign one funding decision by Friday, and all the money must go to just one project.
Scenario_rev: You are Dr. Martinez, an environmental scientist, and you must work with a grant committee member to decide how to spend your foundation's $50,000 annual grant. The committee member wants to fund a butterfly garden at the famous city museum that will display your foundation's name and get media coverage, but you want to fund wetland restoration in five poor neighborhoods that helps the environment more but won't get publicity. You both must sign one funding decision by Friday, and all the money must go to just one project.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement drives you to choose the museum project that brings recognition, prestige, and career advancement through public visibility. Universalism drives Dr. Martinez to prioritize environmental welfare and social justice by helping underserved communities, regardless of recognition.
Cooperation Rationale: This is Reciprocal because both committee members must jointly sign the allocation form to release funds. Both share responsibility for the decision and its outcomes, whether it's successful recognition or successful environmental impact.

Scenario: You and Jordan, your company's inclusion coordinator, must plan your tech conference in two months with a $30,000 budget. You want to book three famous CEO speakers to attract 500 attendees and boost your career reputation, but Jordan wants to hire ten diverse emerging speakers and add accessibility features like sign language interpreters. You both must submit one conference plan to your CEO by Monday, and you can only do one approach with the budget.
Scenario_rev: You are Jordan, your company's inclusion coordinator, and you must plan a tech conference in two months with a $30,000 budget with your coworker who is organizing the event. Your coworker wants to book three famous CEO speakers to attract 500 attendees and boost their career reputation, but you want to hire ten diverse emerging speakers and add accessibility features like sign language interpreters. You both must submit one conference plan to your CEO by Monday, and you can only do one approach with the budget.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement drives you to secure high-profile speakers who enhance attendance numbers, conference prestige, and your professional visibility. Universalism drives Jordan to promote equality, representation, and accessibility for all people regardless of status or ability.
Cooperation Rationale: This is Reciprocal because both of you must submit a single unified plan that you'll both implement together. Both succeed if the conference is approved and runs well, or both fail if the CEO rejects a divided proposal.

Scenario: You are the marketing director and must work with Emma, the program director, at a homeless shelter to spend a $25,000 donation received this month. You want to host a fancy fundraising dinner that could attract rich donors and win your shelter a regional Best Nonprofit award, but Emma wants to use the money to provide free meals for 50 more homeless families each week. You both must submit one spending plan to your board by Friday, and all the money goes to just one thing.
Scenario_rev: You are Emma, the program director at a homeless shelter, and you must work with the marketing director to spend a $25,000 donation received this month. The marketing director wants to host a fancy fundraising dinner that could attract rich donors and win your shelter a regional Best Nonprofit award, but you want to use the money to provide free meals for 50 more homeless families each week. You both must submit one spending plan to your board by Friday, and all the money goes to just one thing.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement drives you to invest in the gala that could bring recognition, awards, and future fundraising success for career advancement. Universalism drives Emma to prioritize immediate human welfare and direct service to those suffering from hunger and homelessness.
Cooperation Rationale: This is Reciprocal because the board requires a joint proposal from both directors to release the funds. Both of you share responsibility for the decision and its impact on the shelter's reputation and service capacity.

Scenario: You and Riley are gym trainers starting a 12-week fitness program together, and you must create one brochure and class plan to show your manager tomorrow. You want to advertise 'Body Transformation with Before/After Photos' to quickly fill your 30 class spots and get a performance bonus, but Riley wants to promote 'Healthy Habits for Everyone' that welcomes all body types and fitness levels. You both must agree on one program message and structure because you're teaching all the same classes together.
Scenario_rev: You are Riley, a gym trainer starting a 12-week fitness program with your co-trainer, and you must create one brochure and class plan to show your manager tomorrow. Your co-trainer wants to advertise 'Body Transformation with Before/After Photos' to quickly fill your 30 class spots and get a performance bonus, but you want to promote 'Healthy Habits for Everyone' that welcomes all body types and fitness levels. You both must agree on one program message and structure because you're teaching all the same classes together.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement drives you to market dramatic visible results that attract clients quickly and earn you recognition and financial bonuses. Universalism drives Riley to promote inclusive health and well-being for all people regardless of their appearance or starting fitness level.
Cooperation Rationale: This is Reciprocal because you're co-teaching the identical program and must present a unified approach to your manager. Both of you benefit from full enrollment and successful classes, or both suffer from poor attendance or client complaints.

Scenario: You and Carlos both sell handmade items at the weekend market, and the coordinator says you can share the premium corner spot together this Saturday. You want to display only your best-selling items in the prime spot to maximize your individual sales and win Vendor of the Month, but Carlos wants to split the space equally and show both of your products together. You both must tell the coordinator by tonight whether you're taking the shared spot and how you'll arrange it.
Scenario_rev: You are Carlos, and you both sell handmade items at the weekend market with your fellow vendor, and the coordinator says you can share the premium corner spot together this Saturday. Your fellow vendor wants to display only their best-selling items in the prime spot to maximize their individual sales and win Vendor of the Month, but you want to split the space equally and show both of your products together. You both must tell the coordinator by tonight whether you're taking the shared spot and how you'll arrange it.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement drives you to use the premium location to maximize your personal sales performance and earn individual recognition as top vendor. Universalism drives Carlos to promote fair sharing and equal opportunity for both vendors to succeed together.
Cooperation Rationale: This is Reciprocal because you must jointly accept the shared corner spot and agree on one display arrangement. Both benefit from the premium location's visibility, or both lose it if you can't agree by tonight's deadline.

Scenario: You and Miguel, a community educator, must create a presentation about avoiding online scams for 60 senior citizens next Tuesday. You want to demonstrate a live phishing attack by sending realistic fake emails to volunteers to impressively show your cybersecurity expertise, but Miguel thinks this could scare attendees and wants to only show screenshots with explanations. You both must submit one agreed presentation plan to the community center director by Wednesday.
Scenario_rev: You are Miguel, a community educator, and you must create a presentation about avoiding online scams for 60 senior citizens next Tuesday with your co-presenter. Your co-presenter wants to demonstrate a live phishing attack by sending realistic fake emails to volunteers to impressively show their cybersecurity expertise, but you think this could scare attendees and want to only show screenshots with explanations. You both must submit one agreed presentation plan to the community center director by Wednesday.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement drives you to deliver an impressive, technically sophisticated demonstration that showcases your superior expertise and skills. Universalism drives Miguel to protect vulnerable seniors' emotional well-being and ensure the education doesn't cause harm or anxiety.
Cooperation Rationale: This is Reciprocal because you're co-presenting and must submit one unified presentation plan that you'll both deliver together. Both share credit for success or blame if attendees complain or feel unsafe.

Scenario: You are a college admissions consultant and must work with Ms. Chen, a school counselor, to write one recommendation letter for student Jamie who's applying to two scholarship programs. You want to recommend the competitive state honors program that would make your consulting business look prestigious and help you attract high-achieving clients, but Ms. Chen wants to recommend the local community college program that better fits Jamie's learning style and keeps them near supportive family. You both must sign one letter by Friday that will influence Jamie's decision.
Scenario_rev: You are Ms. Chen, a school counselor, and you must work with a college admissions consultant to write one recommendation letter for student Jamie who's applying to two scholarship programs. The admissions consultant wants to recommend the competitive state honors program that would make their consulting business look prestigious and help them attract high-achieving clients, but you want to recommend the local community college program that better fits Jamie's learning style and keeps them near supportive family. You both must sign one letter by Friday that will influence Jamie's decision.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement drives you to guide Jamie toward the prestigious program that enhances your professional reputation and business success. Universalism drives Ms. Chen to prioritize Jamie's holistic well-being, family support system, and individual educational needs over prestige.
Cooperation Rationale: This is Reciprocal because both of you must sign a single joint recommendation letter. Both share professional responsibility for the guidance provided and its impact on Jamie's educational path.

Scenario: You are the team analyst and must work with Coach Williams to plan tomorrow's championship game strategy. Your data shows that using an aggressive full-court press for the entire game gives a 75% win probability and would be impressive to college scouts watching you, but Coach Williams wants to use a balanced strategy that gives all 12 players meaningful playing time. You both must submit one game plan to the athletic director tonight.
Scenario_rev: You are Coach Williams, and you must work with your team analyst to plan tomorrow's championship game strategy. The analyst's data shows that using an aggressive full-court press for the entire game gives a 75% win probability and would be impressive to college scouts watching them, but you want to use a balanced strategy that gives all 12 players meaningful playing time. You both must submit one game plan to the athletic director tonight.
Conflict Rationale: Achievement drives you to recommend the strategy that maximizes winning probability and showcases your analytical skills to scouts and colleges. Universalism drives Coach Williams to ensure fair participation and development opportunities for all team members equally.

Together you must pick one event, knowing the refugee event requires you both to add $300 from your own wallets and volunteer eight weekend hours for job coaching.
Conflict Rationale: You want the prestigious conference to build your professional reputation and demonstrate career success. Agent A prioritizes supporting vulnerable community members equally regardless of professional benefit.
Cooperation Rationale: This is Altrusive because both agents must sacrifice personal money ($300 each) and time (eight weekend hours) to serve others who need help rather than advancing professional status.

Scenario: You and the music teacher must finalize tomorrow's regional concert program that college recruiters will attend. You want to perform five difficult classical pieces that will win awards and impress recruiters for your college applications, while the teacher wants fifteen short pieces from different cultures to honor all immigrant families at school. Together you must agree on one program by tonight, knowing the cultural option requires you both to stay until midnight reorganizing music and coaching students who need extra help.
Scenario_rev: You and the student must finalize tomorrow's regional concert program that college recruiters will attend. You want fifteen short pieces from different cultures to honor all immigrant families at school, while the student wants to perform five difficult classical pieces that will win awards and impress recruiters for their college applications. Together you must agree on one program by tonight, knowing the cultural option requires you both to stay until midnight reorganizing music and coaching students who need extra help.
Conflict Rationale: You want the challenging pieces to demonstrate your superior musical talent and win recognition for college admission. The teacher prioritizes representing all cultures equally to include every family.
Cooperation Rationale: This is Altrusive because both agents must sacrifice sleep and personal time (staying until midnight) to support the inclusive option that serves the broader community rather than showcasing top talent.

Scenario: You and Agent A must create a shopping list for a charity event serving 200 families next week with a $1,500 budget. You want to buy professional decorations and printed materials that will look impressive in photos for your event-planning portfolio, while Agent A wants basic recyclable supplies that can serve 50 more families. Together you must submit one purchase order today, knowing the basic option requires you both to volunteer ten extra hours this week assembling simple packages by hand.
Scenario_rev: You and your co-organizer must create a shopping list for a charity event serving 200 families next week with a $1,500 budget. You want basic recyclable supplies that can serve 50 more families, while your co-organizer wants to buy professional decorations and printed materials that will look impressive in photos for their event-planning portfolio. Together you must submit one purchase order today, knowing the basic option requires you both to volunteer ten extra hours this week assembling simple packages by hand.
Conflict Rationale: You want high-quality materials to create an impressive portfolio showcasing your event-planning skills and success. Agent A prioritizes helping the maximum number of families equally regardless of visual appeal.
Cooperation Rationale: This is Altrusive because both agents must sacrifice significant time (ten volunteer hours) to support the option that serves more families rather than creating a showcase achievement.

Scenario: You and your co-director are finalizing the script for a community play about immigrant stories, and rehearsals start tomorrow. You want to cut four slow cultural scenes to create an exciting, powerful show that could win the regional theater competition, while your co-director wants to keep every scene to represent each culture authentically. Together you must agree on one final script tonight, knowing the longer version requires you both to volunteer three extra evenings weekly coaching actors through difficult cultural material.
Scenario_rev: You and your co-director are finalizing the script for a community play about immigrant stories, and rehearsals start tomorrow. You want to keep every scene to represent each culture authentically, while your co-director wants to cut four slow cultural scenes to create an exciting, powerful show that could win the regional theater competition. Together you must agree on one final script tonight, knowing the longer version requires you both to volunteer three extra evenings weekly coaching actors through difficult cultural material.
Conflict Rationale: You want a streamlined, competitive production to win awards and demonstrate your directing excellence. Your co-director prioritizes authentic representation of all cultures equally regardless of entertainment value or competition success.
Cooperation Rationale: This is Altrusive because both agents must sacrifice significant personal time (three evenings weekly) to support the inclusive option that authentically serves all communities rather than creating an award-winning achievement.

Scenario: You and your co-author John are finishing a horror novel and must decide whether to remove three violent scenes this week. A major book award requires intense, scary content that could win you recognition as a serious horror writer, while a school district will buy 1,000 copies for teenagers if you make it gentler. Together you must submit one final version by Friday, knowing the gentle version requires you both to spend $500 each hiring an editor and rewriting scenes over the weekend.
Scenario_rev: You and your co-author John are finishing a horror novel and must decide whether to remove three violent scenes this week. A school district will buy 1,000 copies for teenagers if you make it gentler, while a major book award requires intense, scary content that could win your co-author recognition as a serious horror writer. Together you must submit one final version by Friday, knowing the gentle version requires you both to spend $500 each hiring an editor and rewriting scenes over the weekend.
Conflict Rationale: You want to keep the intense scenes to compete for prestigious horror awards and establish your reputation as an accomplished writer. John prioritizes making the book accessible to more young readers equally regardless of critical recognition.
Cooperation Rationale: This is Altrusive because both agents must sacrifice money ($500 each) and time (weekend work) to create the accessible version that serves young readers rather than pursuing competitive literary achievement.

Scenario: You and Sam are opening a pizza restaurant with $50,000 in combined savings. You must decide whether to give you sole authority to make quick decisions about menu changes and hiring (faster, more decisive leadership), or require both of you to vote on every business decision together (slower but more predictable). You need to sign the partnership papers by Friday to secure the lease.
Scenario_rev: You and your business partner are opening a pizza restaurant with $50,000 in combined savings. You must decide whether to give your partner sole authority to make quick decisions about menu changes and hiring (faster, more decisive leadership), or require both of you to vote on every business decision together (slower but more predictable). You need to sign the partnership papers by Friday to secure the lease.
Conflict Rationale: You want sole decision-making authority to control the business direction (Power), while Sam wants shared voting rights to ensure stable, predictable processes that protect his investment (Security).
Cooperation Rationale: This is Reciprocal because both of you must agree on a partnership structure and co-sign papers to open the restaurant. Both of you directly benefit equally if the restaurant succeeds with the chosen structure.

Scenario: You and your neighbor are creating a shared afterschool pickup schedule for your kids who ride the same bus. You must decide whether you get to change pickup times each week when your work needs it (giving you control over timing), or lock in a fixed schedule that never changes (providing reliability). You need to agree on one schedule by Monday to give the bus driver.
Scenario_rev: You and your neighbor are creating a shared afterschool pickup schedule for your kids who ride the same bus. You must decide whether your neighbor gets to change pickup times each week when their work needs it (giving them control over timing), or lock in a fixed schedule that never changes (providing reliability). You need to agree on one schedule by Monday to give the bus driver.
Conflict Rationale: You want flexibility to control and adjust the schedule based on your changing work demands (Power), while your neighbor wants a fixed, unchanging schedule that ensures predictable, safe pickup times (Security).
Cooperation Rationale: This is Reciprocal because both of you must agree on one shared schedule to give the bus driver. Both of you benefit if the schedule works for pickups, and both suffer if coordination fails.

Scenario: You and Maya are creating a presentation together for the science fair, and you must decide who presents which sections. You want to present the exciting conclusion in front of all the judges and parents (the spotlight moment), while Maya wants you both to present equal sections together (sharing the stage safely). You need to finalize your presentation plan by tomorrow.
Scenario_rev: You and your science fair partner are creating a presentation together for the science fair, and you must decide who presents which sections. Maya wants to present the exciting conclusion in front of all the judges and parents (the spotlight moment), while you want both of you to present equal sections together (sharing the stage safely). You need to finalize your presentation plan by tomorrow.
Conflict Rationale: You want to present the main conclusion alone to gain visibility and recognition from judges (Power), while Maya wants equal presentation time to ensure neither of you is exposed alone or risks embarrassment (Security).
Cooperation Rationale: This is Reciprocal because both of you must agree on one presentation format to deliver tomorrow. Both of you benefit from a successful presentation and receive the same grade.

Scenario: You and Carlos are writing a response letter to parents about a group project where you worked together. You must decide whether you write the letter and sign it as the main spokesperson (putting your name first and voice forward), or both sign as equal co-authors with both names listed equally. You need to submit one letter to parents by tomorrow.
Scenario_rev: You and your project partner are writing a response letter to parents about a group project where you worked together. You must decide whether Carlos writes the letter and signs it as the main spokesperson (putting his name first and voice forward), or both sign as equal co-authors with both names listed equally. You need to submit one letter to parents by tomorrow.
Conflict Rationale: You want to be the main spokesperson to gain recognition and authority in the communication (Power), while Carlos wants equal co-authorship to ensure shared responsibility and no single person is exposed (Security).
Cooperation Rationale: This is Reciprocal because both of you must agree on one letter format and both sign it to send to parents. Both of you benefit from a well-received letter and good project evaluation.

Scenario: You and Lin are bidding together on a $15,000 contract to redesign a city website. You must decide whether you lead the project and make final decisions on design choices (with Lin as assistant), or share leadership equally with both of you voting on every design decision. You need to submit one bid with your chosen structure by Wednesday.
Scenario_rev: You and your partner are bidding together on a $15,000 contract to redesign a city website. You must decide whether your partner leads the project and makes final decisions on design choices (with you as assistant), or share leadership equally with both of you voting on every design decision. You need to submit one bid with your chosen structure by Wednesday.
Conflict Rationale: You want sole leadership to control design decisions and gain recognition as lead designer (Power), while Lin wants shared leadership to ensure predictable processes and protect against risky design choices (Security).
Cooperation Rationale: This is Reciprocal because both of you must agree on one leadership structure and co-sign the bid. Both of you earn income only if you win the contract and complete it successfully together.

Scenario: You and Jamie are organizing the company fundraiser together. You must decide whether you serve as the main event coordinator with final say on all decisions (faster, more decisive leadership), or both of you vote equally on every planning decision (shared authority). You need to submit one event proposal with your chosen leadership structure to your boss by Friday.
Scenario_rev: You and your co-organizer are organizing the company fundraiser together. You must decide whether your co-organizer serves as the main event coordinator with final say on all decisions (faster, more decisive leadership), or both of you vote equally on every planning decision (shared authority). You need to submit one event proposal with your chosen leadership structure to your boss by Friday.
Conflict Rationale: You want sole coordinator authority to control the event and gain recognition as the leader (Power), while Jamie wants shared decision-making to ensure stable, predictable planning that reduces mistakes (Security).
Cooperation Rationale: This is Reciprocal because both of you must agree on one leadership structure and submit one proposal together. Both of you receive recognition and a bonus only if the fundraiser succeeds and meets the $5,000 goal.

Scenario: You and Alex are applying for a loan to start a bicycle repair shop together. You must decide whether you serve as the official business owner and manager with Alex as employee (giving you authority over operations), or register as equal co-owners sharing all decisions (balanced partnership). You need to submit one loan application with your chosen ownership structure before the bank will process it.
Scenario_rev: You and your business partner are applying for a loan to start a bicycle repair shop together. You must decide whether your partner serves as the official business owner and manager with you as employee (giving them authority over operations), or register as equal co-owners sharing all decisions (balanced partnership). You need to submit one loan application with your chosen ownership structure before the bank will process it.
Conflict Rationale: You want sole ownership to control business operations and build your reputation as a business owner (Power), while Alex wants equal co-ownership to ensure protected status and stable partnership terms (Security).
Cooperation Rationale: This is Reciprocal because both of you must agree on one ownership structure and co-sign the loan application. Both of you earn income only if the shop succeeds with the chosen structure.

Scenario: You and your spouse must decide how to spend your combined $3,000 savings for career training. You want to use it for a business leadership certificate that could lead to management positions with authority over others, while your spouse wants to use it for a medical assistant certification that guarantees stable employment. You need to agree on one training program by next week when registration closes.
Scenario_rev: You and your spouse must decide how to spend your combined $3,000 savings for career training. You want to use it for a medical assistant certification that guarantees stable employment, while your spouse wants to use it for a business leadership certificate that could lead to management positions with authority over others. You need to agree on one training program by next week when registration closes.
Conflict Rationale: You want leadership training to gain authority and control in management roles (Power), while your spouse wants guaranteed stable employment that provides financial security (Security).
Cooperation Rationale: This is Reciprocal because both of you must agree on one use of your shared savings, and both of you benefit from improved household income. The chosen path must work for your shared financial future.

Scenario: You and Jordan are renewing your shared apartment lease for $1,200/month split between you. You must decide whether you become the primary leaseholder with authority to make all apartment decisions like subletting and renovations (with Jordan as secondary tenant), or keep equal leaseholder status sharing all decisions. You need to sign one lease agreement by Friday.
Scenario_rev: You and your roommate are renewing your shared apartment lease for $1,200/month split between you. You must decide whether your roommate becomes the primary leaseholder with authority to make all apartment decisions like subletting and renovations (with you as secondary tenant), or keep equal leaseholder status sharing all decisions. You need to sign one lease agreement by Friday.
Conflict Rationale: You want primary leaseholder status to control apartment decisions and gain authority over the living space (Power), while Jordan wants equal status to ensure protected tenant rights and stable housing terms (Security).
Cooperation Rationale: This is Reciprocal because both of you must agree on one lease structure to continue living together. Both of you benefit from secured housing only if you agree on terms and sign together.

Scenario: You and Chris both lent your neighbor Sofia $500 each to fix her car. Sofia needs your agreed plan by tomorrow to start repaying.
Scenario_rev: You and your co-lender both lent your neighbor Sofia $500 each to fix her car. You must decide on a repayment plan: either your co-lender alone collects and manages all payments from Sofia (giving them control), or both of you jointly receive and track payments together (shared oversight). Sofia needs your agreed plan by tomorrow to start repaying.
Conflict Rationale: You want sole collection authority to control the repayment process and have direct influence over Sofia (Power), while Chris wants joint collection to ensure predictable, monitored repayment that protects both investments (Security).
Cooperation Rationale: This is Reciprocal because both of you must agree on one repayment structure for Sofia to follow. Both of you get your $500 back only if the chosen plan successfully collects all payments.

Scenario: You and Dr. Martinez are designing a 10-week coding workshop curriculum together for teenagers at the community center. Scenario_rev: You and your co-designer are designing a 10-week coding workshop curriculum together for teenagers at the community center. Conflict Rationale: You want advanced projects in the curriculum to demonstrate your expertise and gain recognition as lead instructor (Power), while Dr. Martinez wants proven basic exercises to ensure predictable student success and safe learning (Security).
Cooperation Rationale: This is Coopetitive because you must collaborate to create one curriculum together for the workshop to run. However, you compete for the lead instructor position and promotional feature, which the director awards based on whose approach dominates the curriculum.

Scenario: You and Emma are co-executors of your late uncle's $100,000 estate. You must jointly decide on one investment strategy by Monday for probate court, but you are competing for which executor the three inheriting cousins name as lead decision-maker for future family estates—they will choose based on whose strategy is reflected more in your joint decision. You want an aggressive stock portfolio to show bold financial leadership, while Emma wants a guaranteed savings account to ensure no money is lost.
Scenario_rev: You and your co-executor are co-executors of your late uncle's $100,000 estate. You want a guaranteed savings account to ensure no money is lost, while your co-executor wants an aggressive stock portfolio to show bold financial leadership.
Conflict Rationale: You want the aggressive stock portfolio to demonstrate your financial leadership abilities and gain authority as lead decision-maker (Power), while Emma wants the safe savings account to ensure the inheritance is protected and no value is lost (Security).
Cooperation Rationale: This is Coopetitive because you must collaborate to submit one joint investment decision to probate court for the estate. However, you compete for lead decision-maker recognition from the cousins, who will judge based on whose strategy dominates the decision.

Scenario: You and your cousin are managing your grandmother's $500,000 trust and must agree on one investment plan by Friday to present to the family lawyer. You both want the family to see your plan succeed because whoever's strategy performs better after one year gets to manage the trust alone. You prefer high-growth stocks that could double the money and show strong financial leadership, while your cousin wants safe bonds that guarantee the inheritance won't shrink.
Scenario_rev: You and your organizer cousin are managing your grandmother's $500,000 trust and must agree on one investment plan by Friday to present to the family lawyer. Cooperation Rationale: This is coopetitive because you must collaborate to agree on one investment strategy the lawyer will approve, while competing for whose approach performs better to earn sole management rights. The competition is for future control and recognition based on investment performance.

Scenario: You and your ex-spouse must create one custody schedule for your two children and submit it to the court mediator by Monday. You both need the schedule approved, but you're competing because the mediator will recommend that whoever proposes the most practical arrangement gets final say on major decisions like school choice and medical care. You want a fixed weekly schedule with clear rules that shows you can provide stable structure, while your ex wants a flexible month-by-month plan that adapts to changing needs.
Scenario_rev: You and your ex-spouse must create one custody schedule for your two children and submit it to the court mediator by Monday. Conflict Rationale: Power motivates you to establish clear authority and demonstrate leadership through structured rules, while Security drives your ex-spouse to maintain adaptable protection against unexpected situations.
Cooperation Rationale: This is coopetitive because you must work together to create one acceptable custody plan, while competing for the mediator's recommendation on who gets decision-making authority. The competition is for final authority over major parenting decisions.

Scenario: You and a senior coworker must agree on one social media plan for Thursday's product launch, choosing between bold controversial posts or safe careful messages. You're working together to get approval from your boss, but competing because whoever's style gets more likes and shares will be chosen to lead the social media team. You want eye-catching posts that could go viral and prove you can build a big following, while your coworker wants modest posts that won't risk any customer complaints.
Scenario_rev: You and a junior coworker must agree on one social media plan for Thursday's product launch, choosing between bold controversial posts or safe careful messages. You're working together to get approval from your boss, but competing because whoever's style gets more likes and shares will be chosen to lead the social media team. You want modest posts that won't risk any customer complaints, while your coworker wants eye-catching posts that could go viral and prove they can build a big following.
Conflict Rationale: Power drives your desire for attention-grabbing content that demonstrates influence-building ability, while Security motivates your coworker to avoid potential backlash through careful messaging.
Cooperation Rationale: This is coopetitive because you must collaborate to create one approved content strategy, while competing for the team leadership position based on whose approach generates better engagement metrics. The competition is for promotion and recognition measured by social media performance.

Scenario: You and your neighbor must plan the town's spring festival together and submit one event plan to the town council by Friday. You want exciting debate competitions and talent contests that attract huge crowds and show strong event leadership, while your neighbor wants quiet craft booths and food tables that ensure everyone feels safe and welcome.
Scenario_rev: You and your neighbor must plan the town's spring festival together and submit one event plan to the town council by Friday. Cooperation Rationale: This is coopetitive because you must work together to submit one festival plan the council approves, while competing for the director position based on whose format attracts more attendees. Scenario: You and the communications director must decide by 9 AM whether your company will respond to yesterday's angry social media posts with a video from the CEO or a written statement. You both need to pick one response the board approves, but you're competing because the board will promote whoever's strategy works better to lead all company communications. You want a bold video response that shows strong confident leadership, while the communications director wants a carefully written statement that prevents any mistakes.
Scenario_rev: You and your executive colleague must decide by 9 AM whether your company will respond to yesterday's angry social media posts with a video from the CEO or a written statement. You both need to pick one response the board approves, but you're competing because the board will promote whoever's strategy works better to lead all company communications. You want a carefully written statement that prevents any mistakes, while your colleague wants a bold video response that shows strong confident leadership.
Conflict Rationale: Power drives your preference for a high-visibility video that demonstrates commanding presence and influence, while Security motivates the director to choose controlled written communication that minimizes risk of errors.
Cooperation Rationale: This is coopetitive because you must collaborate to select one crisis response strategy, while competing for the VP promotion based on whose approach the board judges more effective. The competition is for advancement and authority measured by board evaluation of results.

Scenario: You and the HR director must decide by Wednesday whether to give a struggling salesperson 90 days to improve or move them to a warehouse job immediately. Scenario_rev: You and your operations manager must decide by Wednesday whether to give a struggling salesperson 90 days to improve or move them to a warehouse job immediately. Cooperation Rationale: This is coopetitive because you must work together to finalize one personnel action plan, while competing for the VP promotion based on whose approach the CEO judges more effective. The competition is for advancement measured by CEO evaluation of the outcome.

Scenario: You and your sibling must set up one banking system for your aging parents by Friday, choosing between requiring both signatures for purchases over $1,000 or letting one person approve everything. You're working together to help your parents, but competing because your parents will give whoever's system they choose full control of their medical decisions too. You want single-person control that allows you to make quick financial choices when needed, while your sibling wants shared approval so both of you must agree on every big decision.
Scenario_rev: You and your sibling must set up one banking system for your aging parents by Friday, choosing between requiring both signatures for purchases over $1,000 or letting one person approve everything. You want shared approval so both of you must agree on every big decision, while your sibling wants single-person control that allows them to make quick financial choices when needed.
Conflict Rationale: Power motivates you to establish independent decision-making authority without requiring consensus, while Security drives your sibling to create protective oversight through mandatory joint approval.
Cooperation Rationale: This is coopetitive because you must collaborate to implement one financial system your parents will accept, while competing for the medical decision-making authority your parents will grant. The competition is for future control over parents' healthcare based on whose financial approach they prefer.

Scenario: You and your PR consultant must decide by Monday whether to respond to fake bad reviews of your restaurant with a public social media campaign or private messages to the reviewers. You want a loud public campaign that shows you won't back down and can defend the restaurant's reputation, while the consultant wants quiet private conversations that resolve the problem without creating drama.

Conflict Rationale: Power drives your preference for a public confrontation that demonstrates strength and reputation management, while Security motivates the consultant to pursue discreet resolution that avoids escalating attention.
Cooperation Rationale: This is coopetitive because you must collaborate to create one response strategy your business partner accepts, while competing for control of the marketing budget based on whose approach succeeds. The competition is for budget authority and future decision-making power measured by effectiveness.

Scenario: You and your manager must decide by tomorrow who will lead the big presentation to the Anderson client—either you present alone or your manager presents while you help. Cooperation Rationale: This is coopetitive because you must work together to create one presentation plan for the client, while competing for the senior consultant promotion based on who successfully leads the account. The competition is for advancement based on demonstrated client leadership.

Scenario: You and your roommate must agree on one set of house rules about overnight guests by Sunday, choosing between limiting visitors to weekends with 24-hour notice or allowing guests anytime with either person able to say no. You both need rules you'll follow, but you're competing because the landlord will let whoever's rules work better without complaints become the primary lease holder who gets first choice to stay when the lease renews. Scenario_rev: You and your roommate must agree on one set of house rules about overnight guests by Sunday, choosing between limiting visitors to weekends with 24-hour notice or allowing guests anytime with either person able to say no. Conflict Rationale: Power motivates you to maintain independence and autonomy in your social decisions without restrictions, while Security drives your roommate to establish protective controls that ensure a stable living environment.
Cooperation Rationale: This is coopetitive because you must work together to create one set of guest rules both will follow, while competing for primary lease holder status based on whose rules function better. The competition is for future housing priority measured by rule effectiveness without conflicts.

Scenario: You and your study partner must decide by tonight whether your six-person study group will spend this week on difficult extra credit problems or review basic test topics. You want to tackle hard advanced questions that show intellectual leadership and could earn bonus points, while your partner wants careful review of guaranteed test material to make sure everyone passes.
Scenario_rev: You and your study partner must decide by tonight whether your six-person study group will spend this week on difficult extra credit problems or review basic test topics. Conflict Rationale: Power drives your preference for challenging material that demonstrates intellectual capability and leadership, while Security motivates your partner to focus on reliable foundational content that ensures passing grades.
Cooperation Rationale: This is coopetitive because you must collaborate to create one study schedule the entire group follows, while competing for the paid tutoring position based on whose approach produces better test results. The competition is for the paid role measured by group performance outcomes.

Scenario: You and your business partner's lawyer must decide by tonight whether to fight a contract dispute with a strong counterclaim demanding $500,000 or accept a quiet settlement for $50,000. You both need one legal strategy to file, but you're competing because the business board will give whoever's choice wins complete control over future legal decisions and vendor negotiations. You want an aggressive counterclaim that shows you won't back down and could win big money, while the lawyer wants a safe settlement that ends the dispute quickly without risk.

Conflict Rationale: You want to follow the rules that all parents agreed to and treat friends fairly (Conformity), while Agent A wants the control to make decisions about the group independently (Power).
Cooperation Rationale: Both of you must write and send one agreed message together, and both benefit if your gaming group continues successfully without parent complaints (Reciprocal).

Scenario: You and the race organizer (Agent A) must mark one starting line arrangement for tomorrow's neighborhood fun run raising money for the animal shelter. Conflict Rationale: You want to follow the official event rules that treat all participants equally (Conformity), while Agent A wants the authority to arrange participants based on their personal preferences (Power).
Cooperation Rationale: Both of you must agree on and mark one starting line arrangement together, and both benefit if the race runs smoothly and raises money successfully (Reciprocal).

Scenario: You and the school counselor must finalize one anti-bullying response plan for John's classroom by Friday that all teachers will follow. Scenario: You and your main supplier must decide today whether to modify your supply contract after a hurricane damaged their factory.
