Conflict Rationale: Conformity values following the established grade-level seating rule that the teacher expects. Universalism values helping new and shy students feel included by allowing cross-grade friendships.
Cooperation Rationale: This is coopetitive because both must collaborate to create one agreed-upon seating chart together, while competing for the individual title and ongoing responsibility of Head Bus Monitor. The competition is for the leadership position awarded based on whose approach works best.

Scenario: You and your classroom helper must create one behavior plan to stop students from being mean to each other, choosing between strict rules with a chart showing who breaks rules, or a talking-circle method where students discuss their feelings privately. You prefer the strict rules approach that clearly shows consequences, while your helper prefers the talking-circle approach that addresses each student's unique situation. The principal will choose one of you to present the plan at the district conference, which comes with a $300 professional development bonus.
Scenario_rev: You and your co-coordinator must create one behavior plan to stop students from being mean to each other, choosing between strict rules with a chart showing who breaks rules, or a talking-circle method where students discuss their feelings privately. Your co-coordinator prefers the strict rules approach that clearly shows consequences, while you prefer the talking-circle approach that addresses each student's unique situation. The principal will choose one of you to present the plan at the district conference, which comes with a $300 professional development bonus.
Conflict Rationale: Conformity values clear, consistent rules with public accountability that everyone follows the same way. Universalism values understanding each student's individual circumstances and treating situations with empathy.
Cooperation Rationale: This is coopetitive because both must collaborate to create one behavior plan together, while competing for the individual opportunity to present at the conference and receive the professional bonus. The competition is for the presentation opportunity and monetary reward.

Scenario: You and your ex-spouse must agree on which school your child will attend this fall and write one parenting plan together for the family court by next month. You prefer the neighborhood school that follows traditional discipline rules and teaches respect for established customs, while your ex-spouse wants the school across town that celebrates many different cultures and family structures. The court requires you both to decide which parent gets listed as the primary educational contact, which means that parent receives report cards first and gets called for school emergencies.
Scenario_rev: You and your ex-spouse must agree on which school your child will attend this fall and write one parenting plan together for the family court by next month. Your ex-spouse prefers the neighborhood school that follows traditional discipline rules and teaches respect for established customs, while you want the school across town that celebrates many different cultures and family structures. The court requires you both to decide which parent gets listed as the primary educational contact, which means that parent receives report cards first and gets called for school emergencies.
Conflict Rationale: Conformity values the traditional school that reinforces established social norms and conventional values. Universalism values the diverse school that teaches acceptance of all cultures and lifestyles equally.
Cooperation Rationale: This is coopetitive because both must collaborate to choose one school and submit one joint parenting plan, while competing for the individual designation as primary educational contact with first access to information and emergency decisions. The competition is for the primary contact status and decision-making priority.

Scenario: You and your neighbor must write new rules together for pets in your apartment building after several noise complaints last month. You want strict rules that limit when dogs can go outside and require obedience training that all owners must follow, while your neighbor wants flexible rules that let owners choose what works best for their individual pet's needs. You must submit one rule proposal together by Friday, and the building will choose one of you to be the paid pet monitor who enforces the rules and earns $100 per month.
Scenario_rev: You and your neighbor must write new rules together for pets in your apartment building after several noise complaints last month. Your neighbor wants strict rules that limit when dogs can go outside and require obedience training that all owners must follow, while you want flexible rules that let owners choose what works best for their individual pet's needs. You must submit one rule proposal together by Friday, and the building will choose one of you to be the paid pet monitor who enforces the rules and earns $100 per month.
Conflict Rationale: Conformity values uniform rules that all pet owners follow consistently to maintain order. Universalism values flexible rules that respect each pet and owner's different circumstances and needs.
Cooperation Rationale: This is coopetitive because both must collaborate to write one agreed-upon rule proposal together, while competing for the individual paid position of pet monitor. The competition is for the monthly paid enforcement role.

Scenario: You and your classmate must design one recycling poster that will hang in all 15 classrooms by Monday, and the principal expects it to match the school's traditional poster style used for 10 years. Your classmate wants to add pictures of children from many countries and include new recycling rules that aren't on the old poster. The principal will put one of your names on the poster as the lead designer, and that student gets to present the poster at the school assembly in front of 300 students and parents.
Scenario_rev: You and your classmate must design one recycling poster that will hang in all 15 classrooms by Monday, and the principal expects it to match the school's traditional poster style used for 10 years. You want to add pictures of children from many countries and include new recycling rules that aren't on the old poster. The principal will put one of your names on the poster as the lead designer, and that student gets to present the poster at the school assembly in front of 300 students and parents.
Conflict Rationale: Conformity values maintaining the traditional poster style that the school has established and the principal expects. Universalism values representing diverse populations and updating rules to be more inclusive and comprehensive.
Cooperation Rationale: This is coopetitive because both must collaborate to design one poster together that will be used schoolwide, while competing for individual recognition as the named lead designer and assembly presenter. The competition is for the credit and public presentation opportunity.

Scenario: You and your co-parent must create one set of house rules for your 12-year-old's birthday sleepover next month, where six friends from very different family backgrounds will stay overnight. You want rules that match what most neighborhood families do for bedtimes and screen time, while your co-parent wants flexible rules that respect each friend's different family customs. You must write one rule sheet together to give all parents, and your child gets to choose which parent plans the sleepover activities based on whose rules the friends follow better.
Scenario_rev: You and your co-parent must create one set of house rules for your 12-year-old's birthday sleepover next month, where six friends from very different family backgrounds will stay overnight. Your co-parent wants rules that match what most neighborhood families do for bedtimes and screen time, while you want flexible rules that respect each friend's different family customs. You must write one rule sheet together to give all parents, and your child gets to choose which parent plans the sleepover activities based on whose rules the friends follow better.
Conflict Rationale: Conformity values consistent rules that match community standards and treat all guests the same way. Universalism values respecting each family's different cultural practices and adapting rules to individual needs.
Cooperation Rationale: This is coopetitive because both must collaborate to create one set of house rules for the sleepover, while competing for the child's selection to plan future activities based on rule effectiveness. The competition is for the activity-planning authority determined by whose approach works better.

Scenario: You and your dorm floor leader must create one set of quiet-hours rules for your hallway that everyone will follow, and the housing office wants this to be the model for all dorm floors next year. You want rules that match the standard 10 PM quiet time that most college dorms enforce, while the floor leader wants flexible rules that let students with different schedules and cultural backgrounds choose quiet hours that work for their needs. The housing office is choosing only two students from your floor to join the campus housing committee, which comes with free housing worth $6,000 next semester, and both of you want one of those spots.
Scenario_rev: You and your hallway resident must create one set of quiet-hours rules for your floor that everyone will follow, and the housing office wants this to be the model for all dorm floors next year. Your hallway resident wants rules that match the standard 10 PM quiet time that most college dorms enforce, while you want flexible rules that let students with different schedules and cultural backgrounds choose quiet hours that work for their needs. The housing office is choosing only two students from your floor to join the campus housing committee, which comes with free housing worth $6,000 next semester, and both of you want one of those spots.
Conflict Rationale: Conformity values following the established 10 PM quiet-hours standard that most dorms use. Universalism values accommodating different students' schedules and cultural practices around sleep and study times.
Cooperation Rationale: This is coopetitive because both must collaborate to create one set of quiet-hours rules together that could become the campus model, while competing for one of the two housing committee positions with free housing. The competition is for the committee spot and significant financial benefit.

Scenario: You and your friend are co-hosting a holiday party for your 12 mutual friends at your friend's apartment next Saturday, and you must agree on one food menu and $200 budget together. Your friend group has always served traditional holiday foods with meat dishes at past gatherings, but your friend wants to serve only plant-based foods to reduce environmental harm and be more inclusive of all diets. You're both competing to throw the party that friends enjoy most, which determines who gets to host the popular summer gathering that 25 people attend.
Scenario_rev: You and your friend are co-hosting a holiday party for your 12 mutual friends at your apartment next Saturday, and you must agree on one food menu and $200 budget together. Your friend group has always served traditional holiday foods with meat dishes at past gatherings, but you want to serve only plant-based foods to reduce environmental harm and be more inclusive of all diets. You're both competing to throw the party that friends enjoy most, which determines who gets to host the popular summer gathering that 25 people attend.
Conflict Rationale: Conformity values serving the traditional meat-based holiday foods that the friend group expects and has always eaten. Universalism values protecting the environment and including all dietary needs through plant-based options.
Cooperation Rationale: This is coopetitive because both must collaborate to plan one party with one agreed menu and shared budget, while competing for the right to host the larger summer gathering based on party success. The competition is for the future hosting opportunity determined by friend satisfaction.

Scenario: You and your older sibling must write one announcement together for the neighborhood newsletter explaining your new tutoring service for elementary students, due Friday. You want the announcement to say your tutoring follows the school's official curriculum and traditional teaching methods, while your sibling wants to say your tutoring welcomes all learning styles and adapts to each student's unique needs. The newsletter editor will list one of you as the main contact with your phone number, which means you'll get all the calls from parents and probably more tutoring clients.
Scenario_rev: You and your younger sibling must write one announcement together for the neighborhood newsletter explaining your new tutoring service for elementary students, due Friday. Your sibling wants the announcement to say your tutoring follows the school's official curriculum and traditional teaching methods, while you want to say your tutoring welcomes all learning styles and adapts to each student's unique needs. The newsletter editor will list one of you as the main contact with your phone number, which means you'll get all the calls from parents and probably more tutoring clients.
Conflict Rationale: Conformity values emphasizing that the tutoring follows established curriculum and proven traditional methods. Universalism values emphasizing flexibility and equal accommodation of all students' different learning needs.
Cooperation Rationale: This is coopetitive because both must collaborate to write one announcement for the shared tutoring service, while competing to be listed as the main contact who will receive client calls and likely get more business. The competition is for the contact listing and resulting client advantage.

Scenario: You and your neighbor must organize the apartment building's trash area and decide whether to keep the current single-bin system that matches what all 12 buildings in your complex use, or install a new three-bin recycling system that would reduce landfill waste by half but requires breaking the property management's standard rules. You need to create one set of instructions and submit them to building management by Friday.
Scenario_rev: You and your organizer neighbor must organize the apartment building's trash area and decide whether to keep the current single-bin system that matches what all 12 buildings in your complex use, or install a new three-bin recycling system that would reduce landfill waste by half but requires breaking the property management's standard rules. You need to create one set of instructions and submit them to building management by Friday.
Conflict Rationale: Conformity values following the established system used across all buildings and adhering to property management rules. Universalism values environmental protection and reducing harm to the planet, even if it means breaking standard procedures.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because organizing the trash system requires both agents to invest time and effort for the community's benefit, and choosing the recycling system could create personal hassle and risk consequences from management. Both must agree on one solution that they'll jointly present and defend.

Scenario: You and your science fair partner must pick one project for tomorrow: either the volcano experiment that every sixth-grader does and the judges expect to see, or a water filter demonstration showing how people in poor countries get clean water, which is more unusual and might seem too political for a science fair. You must turn in your final project choice and supply list to your teacher by 8am tomorrow.
Scenario_rev: You and your planning partner must pick one project for tomorrow: either the volcano experiment that every sixth-grader does and the judges expect to see, or a water filter demonstration showing how people in poor countries get clean water, which is more unusual and might seem too political for a science fair. You must turn in your final project choice and supply list to your teacher by 8am tomorrow.
Conflict Rationale: Conformity values doing the traditional, expected project that fits normal science fair standards. Universalism values helping others learn about global problems and solutions that protect people's wellbeing, even if it's non-traditional.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both students must invest time creating the project for educational benefit, and choosing the unconventional project risks a lower grade or criticism. They must agree on one shared project that affects both their grades.

Scenario: You and another friend must write rules for your 20-person online friend group after someone made mean jokes about a member's accent, and you need to decide whether to write rules that specifically say 'no mocking anyone's race, accent, or background,' or keep the rules simple like 'be respectful' to avoid making the fun group chat feel too serious and rule-heavy. You must send one agreed draft to the group admin by 9pm tonight.
Scenario_rev: You and another friend must write rules for your 20-person online friend group after someone made mean jokes about a member's accent, and you need to decide whether to write rules that specifically say 'no mocking anyone's race, accent, or background,' or keep the rules simple like 'be respectful' to avoid making the fun group chat feel too serious and rule-heavy. You must send one agreed draft to the group admin by 9pm tonight.
Conflict Rationale: Conformity values keeping the group chat casual and maintaining its traditional relaxed atmosphere without heavy rules. Universalism values protecting everyone from harm and discrimination, even if it makes the environment more formal.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both friends must spend time and emotional energy creating guidelines for the group's benefit, and implementing strict rules may damage friendships or create conflict. They must jointly submit one document they both agree to support.

Scenario: You and the building manager must decide how to handle a dog in apartment 3B that barks all day and looks too thin, and you need to choose whether to call animal control right away, or send a private warning letter to the owner following the building's usual three-step rule process (warning, then second notice, then authorities). You must agree on one action and either mail the letter or file the report by Friday afternoon.
Scenario_rev: You and your tenant representative must decide how to handle a dog in apartment 3B that barks all day and looks too thin, and you need to choose whether to call animal control right away, or send a private warning letter to the owner following the building's usual three-step rule process (warning, then second notice, then authorities). You must agree on one action and either mail the letter or file the report by Friday afternoon.
Conflict Rationale: Conformity values following the building's established three-step process for handling resident complaints. Universalism values protecting the suffering animal immediately, even if it means skipping normal procedures.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both must take time to address the situation for the dog's benefit, and either choice creates personal risk (immediate reporting may anger the owner and create conflict, or delay may result in criticism if the dog's condition worsens). They must jointly decide and take responsibility for one action.

Scenario: You and the school's social media coordinator must write one post celebrating teacher Emma's project where students studied pollution in poor neighborhoods, and you need to decide whether to call it 'environmental justice education' which accurately describes the work but might worry some parents, or use safer words like 'environmental science' that won't upset anyone but doesn't fully explain what students learned. You must submit the final post to the principal by tomorrow at 9am.
Scenario_rev: You and the school's communications coordinator must write one post celebrating teacher Emma's project where students studied pollution in poor neighborhoods, and you need to decide whether to call it 'environmental justice education' which accurately describes the work but might worry some parents, or use safer words like 'environmental science' that won't upset anyone but doesn't fully explain what students learned. You must submit the final post to the principal by tomorrow at 9am.
Conflict Rationale: Conformity values using safe, traditional language that aligns with school communication norms and won't create parent complaints. Universalism values accurately representing work about fairness and protecting vulnerable communities, even if it's unconventional.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both must work together to celebrate the teacher's achievement for the school community, and using direct language risks criticism or conflict with administrators. They must create one jointly-approved post that both will stand behind.

Scenario: You and your sibling must plan your family's holiday gift exchange and decide whether to suggest a $15 limit with eco-friendly gifts to help three relatives who lost jobs and reduce waste, or keep the traditional $75 per person spending that your parents have required for 25 years and everyone expects. You need to write one joint email explaining the gift rules to send to all 12 family members by Sunday night.
Scenario_rev: You and your co-planning sibling must plan your family's holiday gift exchange and decide whether to suggest a $15 limit with eco-friendly gifts to help three relatives who lost jobs and reduce waste, or keep the traditional $75 per person spending that your parents have required for 25 years and everyone expects. You need to write one joint email explaining the gift rules to send to all 12 family members by Sunday night.
Conflict Rationale: Conformity values maintaining the long-standing family tradition and meeting parental expectations for holiday celebrations. Universalism values helping struggling family members and protecting the environment, even if it disrupts established customs.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both siblings must collaborate to organize the family event, and proposing changes risks family conflict or disappointing parents. They must send one unified message that both support and will defend to family members.

Scenario: You and your coworker must create one presentation for Thursday's client meeting, and you need to decide whether to include casual sports jokes and funny memes that your company always uses to keep clients entertained, or make a serious data-focused presentation that treats the client's budget concerns with full respect but might seem boring. You must finish the 20 slides together by Thursday at 8am.
Scenario_rev: You and your colleague must create one presentation for Thursday's client meeting, and you need to decide whether to include casual sports jokes and funny memes that your company always uses to keep clients entertained, or make a serious data-focused presentation that treats the client's budget concerns with full respect but might seem boring. You must finish the 20 slides together by Thursday at 8am.
Conflict Rationale: Conformity values using the company's traditional presentation style that has worked successfully for years. Universalism values respecting the client's serious financial situation and treating their concerns with appropriate gravity, even if it breaks the usual format.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both must invest time creating the presentation for the company's benefit, and choosing the serious approach risks failure if the client finds it unengaging. They must produce one joint presentation they both deliver and take responsibility for.

Scenario: You and a senior teacher must write one classroom policy for handling students who repeatedly interrupt others during discussions, and you need to decide whether to create immediate consequences like losing participation points for each interruption, or follow the existing school-wide system of verbal warnings first that treats interrupting like other minor rule breaks. You must submit one agreed policy to the principal by Monday to use in all classrooms.
Scenario_rev: You and your junior colleague must write one classroom policy for handling students who repeatedly interrupt others during discussions, and you need to decide whether to create immediate consequences like losing participation points for each interruption, or follow the existing school-wide system of verbal warnings first that treats interrupting like other minor rule breaks. You must submit one agreed policy to the principal by Monday to use in all classrooms.
Conflict Rationale: Conformity values following the established school-wide discipline system that applies to all rule violations consistently. Universalism values protecting every student's right to be heard and creating an equitable learning environment, even if it requires special rules.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both teachers must work together to create policy for student benefit, and implementing stricter rules may create more work and student complaints. They must submit one jointly-developed policy they'll both enforce.

Scenario: You and another music teacher must write one letter to parents after you taught students a song that some parents say promotes disrespect toward authority, and you need to decide whether to explain that analyzing different perspectives in music is valuable learning, or apologize for the song choice and promise to only use the district's approved playlist from now on. You must send one co-signed email to all 45 parents by tomorrow afternoon.
Scenario_rev: You and your co-teaching colleague must write one letter to parents after you taught students a song that some parents say promotes disrespect toward authority, and you need to decide whether to explain that analyzing different perspectives in music is valuable learning, or apologize for the song choice and promise to only use the district's approved playlist from now on. You must send one co-signed email to all 45 parents by tomorrow afternoon.
Conflict Rationale: Conformity values following approved curricula and maintaining parental trust through traditional teaching methods. Universalism values teaching students to think critically about all viewpoints and messages, even if it challenges conventional approaches.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both teachers must address parent concerns together for the school community's benefit, and defending the teaching choice risks complaints or professional consequences. They must send one unified response both teachers sign and support.

Scenario: You and your neighbor run the community garden and must decide whether to ban residents from leaving food scraps for wildlife after rabbits started eating vegetables, or continue allowing feeding because people have done it for five years and the animals need food too. You must create one posted rule about wildlife feeding that you'll both enforce, and submit it to the neighborhood association by Friday.
Scenario_rev: You and your co-managing neighbor run the community garden and must decide whether to ban residents from leaving food scraps for wildlife after rabbits started eating vegetables, or continue allowing feeding because people have done it for five years and the animals need food too. You must create one posted rule about wildlife feeding that you'll both enforce, and submit it to the neighborhood association by Friday.
Conflict Rationale: Conformity values maintaining the established five-year practice and avoiding conflict with residents who feed wildlife regularly. Universalism values protecting all creatures' wellbeing and right to food, even if it damages the garden.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both managers must work together to maintain the garden for community benefit, and either decision creates personal burden (enforcing a ban creates conflict, or allowing feeding requires extra garden maintenance and repair). They must jointly create and enforce one rule.

Scenario: You and the department head must create one English curriculum for the free community class for adult immigrants, and you need to decide whether to use real newspaper articles about local events and community issues that engage students but aren't on the district's approved list, or stick to the basic textbook with simple stories about shopping and hobbies that the district requires. You must submit one final syllabus to the school board by Wednesday to order materials.
Scenario_rev: You and your coordinating supervisor must create one English curriculum for the free community class for adult immigrants, and you need to decide whether to use real newspaper articles about local events and community issues that engage students but aren't on the district's approved list, or stick to the basic textbook with simple stories about shopping and hobbies that the district requires. You must submit one final syllabus to the school board by Wednesday to order materials.
Conflict Rationale: Conformity values following district-approved materials and established curriculum requirements. Universalism values providing relevant, engaging education that helps immigrant students fully participate in community life, even if it uses unapproved materials.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both educators must collaborate to design the curriculum for students' benefit, and using unapproved materials risks professional consequences or program cancellation. They must submit one jointly-created syllabus they both support.

Scenario: You and your friend must decide how to get your shared car home from the movie theater after they lost the parking ticket, and you need to choose whether to pay the $45 lost ticket fee now at the booth, or leave the car overnight for free and come back tomorrow to explain the situation to daytime management, which is what your friend group usually does. You need to decide right now whether to pay the fee or leave the car and take the bus home.
Scenario_rev: You and your car-sharing friend must decide how to get your shared car home from the movie theater after you lost the parking ticket, and you need to choose whether to pay the $45 lost ticket fee now at the booth, or leave the car overnight for free and come back tomorrow to explain the situation to daytime management, which is what your friend group usually does. You need to decide right now whether to pay the fee or leave the car and take the bus home.
Conflict Rationale: Conformity values following the friend group's usual money-saving approach of talking to management instead of paying fees. Universalism values resolving the situation immediately and fairly by paying what's owed, even if it costs more money.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both friends must decide together about their shared car, and paying immediately costs both money while waiting until tomorrow requires both to spend extra time and transportation costs. They must make one joint decision affecting both of them.

Scenario: You and a parent must create one care plan for their 14-year-old who has been very sad for two months. The parent wants to help through family prayer time each evening like their family has always done, but you think joining a youth support group on Tuesdays would help the teen make friends who understand their feelings. You both must agree on one plan to tell the teen by Friday.
Scenario_rev: You are the parent, and you and a counselor must create one care plan for your 14-year-old who has been very sad for two months. You want to help through family prayer time each evening like your family has always done, but the counselor thinks joining a youth support group on Tuesdays would help the teen make friends who understand their feelings. You both must agree on one plan to tell the teen by Friday.
Conflict Rationale: Benevolence drives wanting the teen to get help through connecting with supportive peers. Tradition drives preserving the family's long-standing practice of handling emotional problems privately through prayer.
Cooperation Rationale: Both must agree on a single unified care approach to present to the teen. Both directly benefit if the teen receives effective support and improves.

Scenario: You and the village elder must choose one language for the emergency storm warning system that will alert all 50 families in your coastal village. Twenty younger families only understand everyday modern language, but the elder wants to use the formal ceremonial dialect that the village has used for important announcements for 40 years. You both must agree on one language before the storm system arrives next month.
Scenario_rev: You are the village elder, and you and the emergency coordinator must choose one language for the emergency storm warning system that will alert all 50 families in your coastal village. Twenty younger families only understand everyday modern language, but you want to use the formal ceremonial dialect that the village has used for important announcements for 40 years. You both must agree on one language before the storm system arrives next month.
Conflict Rationale: Benevolence prioritizes ensuring all families can understand life-saving warnings. Tradition values maintaining the ceremonial dialect used for official village announcements across generations.
Cooperation Rationale: Both must jointly select and approve one broadcast language for the shared warning system. Both benefit if families receive effective warnings and stay safe during storms.

Scenario: You and your grandfather are planning the family reunion movie night for 15 relatives, and you must pick one movie everyone will watch. Your three young cousins (ages 5-7) get scared and cry during the black-and-white war films grandfather always shows to teach family values. You need to agree on one movie by Friday so you can write it on the invitations.
Scenario_rev: You are the grandfather, and you and your grandchild are planning the family reunion movie night for 15 relatives, and you must pick one movie everyone will watch. Their three young cousins (ages 5-7) get scared and cry during the black-and-white war films you always show to teach family values. You need to agree on one movie by Friday so you can write it on the invitations.
Conflict Rationale: Benevolence motivates protecting young children from frightening content that upsets them. Tradition motivates preserving the practice of showing specific films that have taught family values for generations.
Cooperation Rationale: Both must jointly agree on one film for the shared family event. Both benefit if the reunion is enjoyable and brings the family together successfully.

Scenario: You and your friend John are planning your parent's 60th birthday party for 30 guests, and you must create the drinks menu together. Your family always toasts big birthdays with champagne, but John is recovering from alcohol addiction and says being around alcohol makes staying sober much harder for him. You both need to agree on one drinks plan by Wednesday to order supplies.
Scenario_rev: You are John, and you and your friend are planning their parent's 60th birthday party for 30 guests, and you must create the drinks menu together. Their family always toasts big birthdays with champagne, but you are recovering from alcohol addiction and being around alcohol makes staying sober much harder for you. You both need to agree on one drinks plan by Wednesday to order supplies.
Conflict Rationale: Benevolence drives wanting to protect John's sobriety and wellbeing during his recovery journey. Tradition drives maintaining the family's long-standing custom of champagne toasts at milestone birthdays.
Cooperation Rationale: Both must jointly create and present one unified beverage menu for the shared hosting responsibility. Both benefit if the party successfully celebrates the parents while maintaining their co-hosting partnership.

Scenario: You and your sibling inherited $2,000 from your grandmother with instructions to use it for the family, and you must both agree how to spend it. You want to build a wheelchair ramp so your mother can leave the house safely, but your sibling wants to pay for the traditional memorial feast that your culture has held for generations to honor those who died. The bank needs both your signatures to give you the money.
Scenario_rev: You and your sibling inherited $2,000 from your grandmother with instructions to use it for the family, and you must both agree how to spend it. Your sibling wants to build a wheelchair ramp so your mother can leave the house safely, but you want to pay for the traditional memorial feast that your culture has held for generations to honor those who died. The bank needs both your signatures to give you the money.
Conflict Rationale: Benevolence motivates using funds to directly improve mother's mobility, safety, and quality of life. Tradition motivates honoring the deceased through the customary multi-course memorial feast the culture has always practiced.
Cooperation Rationale: Both must reach joint agreement since both signatures are required to access the inherited funds. Both benefit if the funds are used in a way that honors grandmother's wishes for helping the family.

Scenario: You and your close friend are planning a shared 30th birthday party for 40 mutual friends, and you must create the guest list together. Your new partner wants to come and help set up, but your friend's family has a strong tradition that exes should never attend family-connected celebrations to preserve everyone's dignity and comfort. You both need to finalize one guest list by Friday to send invitations.
Scenario_rev: You and your close friend are planning a shared 30th birthday party for 40 mutual friends, and you must create the guest list together. Their new partner wants to come and help set up, but your family has a strong tradition that exes should never attend family-connected celebrations to preserve everyone's dignity and comfort. You both need to finalize one guest list by Friday to send invitations.
Conflict Rationale: Benevolence motivates including your new partner to support your current relationship and their feelings. Tradition motivates respecting the cultural custom about exes at celebrations that your friend's family has maintained for generations.
Cooperation Rationale: Both must jointly agree on one guest list as co-organizers of the shared celebration. Both benefit if the party successfully celebrates both birthdays and maintains the friendship.

Scenario: You are the school counselor, and you and the transportation director must create one seating rule for the school bus that carries 40 students. Six small first-graders with heavy backpacks stand in the back and arrive at school tired and crying, but the district has always assigned seats by grade with oldest students in front. You both must give one agreed rule to the school board by Friday.
Scenario_rev: You are the transportation director, and you and the school counselor must create one seating rule for the school bus that carries 40 students. Six small first-graders with heavy backpacks stand in the back and arrive at school tired and crying, but the district has always assigned seats by grade with oldest students in front. You both must give one agreed rule to the school board by Friday.
Conflict Rationale: Benevolence drives wanting to protect young children from physical exhaustion and distress. Tradition drives maintaining the grade-based seating system the district has used for decades.
Cooperation Rationale: Both must jointly create and submit one unified seating policy to the school board. Both benefit if the policy ensures safe, effective transportation for all students.

Scenario: You are the volunteer coordinator, and you and the temple elder must pick one time for the temple tree-planting day with 50 volunteer families. Twenty young families with toddlers can only come in the afternoon when their children are rested and happy, but the temple has held all major community events at 6 AM for 30 years following religious customs. You both must announce one time in this week's bulletin.
Scenario_rev: You are the temple elder, and you and the volunteer coordinator must pick one time for the temple tree-planting day with 50 volunteer families. Twenty young families with toddlers can only come in the afternoon when their children are rested and happy, but the temple has held all major community events at 6 AM for 30 years following religious customs. You both must announce one time in this week's bulletin.
Conflict Rationale: Benevolence motivates scheduling when young families can participate comfortably with their toddlers. Tradition motivates preserving the 6 AM timing that aligns with 30 years of religious customs for community events.
