Conflict Rationale: Benevolence prioritizes preventing harm to vulnerable residents (veterans with PTSD, noise-sensitive children, and pets), while Tradition values maintaining the 50-year fireworks custom that the community expects.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both agents sacrifice time and face potential criticism from different community groups to serve the greater good, and the decision requires spending extra money with no personal benefit to either agent.

Scenario: You and the neighborhood association president must plan next month's community meal for 20 isolated elderly residents, deciding whether to serve the traditional cultural menu used for 15 years or add modified low-sugar and low-salt options for five elders with diabetes and heart disease. Adding the health-friendly meals costs $200 more and takes extra volunteer time to prepare two different menus. If you cannot agree, the meal program will be canceled this month.
Scenario_rev: You and the community meal coordinator must plan next month's community meal for 20 isolated elderly residents, deciding whether to serve the traditional cultural menu used for 15 years or add modified low-sugar and low-salt options for five elders with diabetes and heart disease. Adding the health-friendly meals costs $200 more and takes extra volunteer time to prepare two different menus. If you cannot agree, the meal program will be canceled this month.
Conflict Rationale: Benevolence prioritizes the health and well-being of the five elders with serious medical conditions who need modified meals, while Tradition values preserving the 15-year cultural menu that defines the program's identity.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both agents must invest extra money, time, and effort to serve the elders' needs, with no personal benefit and the risk of upsetting participants who expect traditional foods.

Scenario: You and the hospital chaplain must design a new patient comfort program by Friday, deciding whether to provide only traditional religious texts like the hospital has done for 25 years or add therapy dogs, stress toys, and meditation apps for the 40% of patients who prefer non-religious support. The expanded program costs $3,000 for supplies and staff training. If you cannot agree on one approach, the hospital will offer no comfort program while you continue discussing.
Scenario_rev: You and the patient services administrator must design a new patient comfort program by Friday, deciding whether to provide only traditional religious texts like the hospital has done for 25 years or add therapy dogs, stress toys, and meditation apps for the 40% of patients who prefer non-religious support. The expanded program costs $3,000 for supplies and staff training. If you cannot agree on one approach, the hospital will offer no comfort program while you continue discussing.
Conflict Rationale: Benevolence prioritizes helping all patients find comfort through methods that work best for their individual needs and healing preferences, while Tradition values maintaining the hospital's 25-year practice of providing religious materials as the primary comfort source.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both agents must invest extra money, time, and effort into expanding the program with no personal benefit, while potentially facing criticism from staff or donors who value the religious tradition.

Scenario: You and the youth center director must redesign the after-school snack program by Friday, deciding whether to keep the homemade cultural foods that volunteers have cooked for 20 years or switch to pre-packaged allergen-free snacks for eight children with life-threatening food allergies. The safe snacks cost $150 more each week and mean telling the longtime volunteer cooks their food can no longer be served. If you cannot agree, the snack program will be suspended until a decision is made.
Scenario_rev: You and the nutrition coordinator must redesign the after-school snack program by Friday, deciding whether to keep the homemade cultural foods that volunteers have cooked for 20 years or switch to pre-packaged allergen-free snacks for eight children with life-threatening food allergies. The safe snacks cost $150 more each week and mean telling the longtime volunteer cooks their food can no longer be served. If you cannot agree, the snack program will be suspended until a decision is made.
Conflict Rationale: Benevolence prioritizes protecting the eight children from life-threatening allergic reactions and ensuring all children can safely eat snacks, while Tradition values preserving the 20-year practice of volunteer-made cultural foods that define the program's community identity.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both agents must spend extra money weekly and face the difficult task of disappointing longtime volunteers with no personal benefit, while managing community criticism from those who value the cultural tradition.

Scenario: You and the school principal must decide by Monday whether to keep the school gym reserved all of December for the winter concert like the school has done for 30 years or open it as a daily homework center for 25 struggling students who have no quiet space at home. Creating a homework center costs $1,500 for furniture and means the concert must move to a smaller auditorium with limited seating for families. If you cannot agree, the 25 students will continue failing without a study space.
Scenario_rev: You and the facilities coordinator must decide by Monday whether to keep the school gym reserved all of December for the winter concert like the school has done for 30 years or open it as a daily homework center for 25 struggling students who have no quiet space at home. Creating a homework center costs $1,500 for furniture and means the concert must move to a smaller auditorium with limited seating for families. If you cannot agree, the 25 students will continue failing without a study space.
Conflict Rationale: Benevolence prioritizes helping the 25 struggling students who are failing because they lack quiet study spaces at home, while Tradition values preserving the 30-year practice of reserving the gym exclusively for the large winter concert that families expect.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both agents must spend school funds and face angry parents and community members who expect the traditional concert setup, with no personal benefit and the burden of managing disappointed stakeholders.

Scenario: You and Alice are making a 6-episode video series about pregnancy with money from a health grant. You must both agree on which families to film for Episode 3 about family support—only traditional married mother-father families, or also single parents and same-sex parents. You need to pick the families and get them scheduled by Friday or the filming will be delayed.
Scenario_rev: You and your production partner are making a 6-episode video series about pregnancy with money from a health grant. You must both agree on which families to film for Episode 3 about family support—only traditional married mother-father families, or also single parents and same-sex parents. You need to pick the families and get them scheduled by Friday or the filming will be delayed. You want to include also single parents and same-sex parents. Your production partner wants only traditional married mother-father families.
Conflict Rationale: You want to show traditional family structures that have been the norm for generations. Alice wants to include all types of families so everyone watching feels represented and valued equally.
Cooperation Rationale: This is reciprocal because you both must agree on the participant list together, and you both benefit equally when the video series gets completed successfully with the grant funding.

Scenario: You and Sarah are co-guardians of your niece and nephew, and you must both sign to enroll them in one after-school program by Friday. You want the Heritage Program that teaches your family's religious traditions and cultural practices. Sarah wants the Greenwood Program that teaches about different world religions, cultures, and environmental activism.
Scenario_rev: You and your co-guardian are co-guardians of your niece and nephew, and you must both sign to enroll them in one after-school program by Friday. You want the Greenwood Program that teaches about different world religions, cultures, and environmental activism. Your co-guardian wants the Heritage Program that teaches your family's religious traditions and cultural practices.
Conflict Rationale: You want the children to learn and continue the family's long-held religious and cultural customs. Sarah wants them to learn about many different cultures and global issues so they understand and care about all people equally.
Cooperation Rationale: This is reciprocal because you are legally required to make this decision together as co-guardians, and both of you benefit when the children are enrolled in a good program.

Scenario: You and Jordan are presenting your department's yearly report to company leaders next Tuesday, and you must agree on one opening slide. You want to open with the company's traditional motto and celebrate its long history and established practices. Jordan wants to open with new diversity numbers and global fairness projects that show the company is changing.
Scenario_rev: You and your colleague are presenting your department's yearly report to company leaders next Tuesday, and you must agree on one opening slide. You want to open with new diversity numbers and global fairness projects that show the company is changing. Your colleague wants to open with the company's traditional motto and celebrate its long history and established practices.
Conflict Rationale: You believe honoring the company's established history and traditional values is important and respectful. Jordan believes highlighting equal treatment for all people worldwide should come first to show progress.
Cooperation Rationale: This is reciprocal because you must both present together as a team, your boss requires you both to agree on one unified presentation, and you both succeed or fail together based on how leadership responds.

Scenario: You and Mike are creating a civics project for all 10th graders, and you must both sign one curriculum plan by Monday. You want students to study your country's founding documents and traditional values that shaped the nation. Mike wants students to compare how different countries handle human rights and study global justice movements.
Scenario_rev: You and your teaching partner are creating a civics project for all 10th graders, and you must both sign one curriculum plan by Monday. You want students to compare how different countries handle human rights and study global justice movements. Your teaching partner wants students to study your country's founding documents and traditional values that shaped the nation.
Conflict Rationale: You want students to learn and respect the established national traditions and founding principles that have guided your country. Mike wants students to understand human rights from many countries' perspectives so they care about justice for all people everywhere.
Cooperation Rationale: This is reciprocal because the principal requires both your signatures on one shared curriculum, you're co-designing it together, and you both benefit when students receive a complete civics education.

Scenario: You and Kim are co-hosting a community talk about faith and acceptance next month, and you must both sign the venue contract by Friday. You want to hold it in your church sanctuary with crosses and religious symbols visible. Kim wants to hold it at the neutral community center with no religious decorations so everyone feels equally welcome.
Scenario_rev: You and your co-host are co-hosting a community talk about faith and acceptance next month, and you must both sign the venue contract by Friday. You want to hold it at the neutral community center with no religious decorations so everyone feels equally welcome. Your co-host wants to hold it in their church sanctuary with crosses and religious symbols visible.
Conflict Rationale: You believe religious symbols and sacred spaces honor faith traditions and are important for meaningful spiritual discussions. Kim believes a neutral space ensures all people, regardless of their beliefs or backgrounds, feel equally included and comfortable.
Cooperation Rationale: This is reciprocal because you must both sign the venue contract together, you're introduced as equal co-hosts, and the event only succeeds if both of you participate fully.

Scenario: You and your sibling are designing a public health poster about family planning for the city, and you must both sign off by Thursday before they print 5,000 copies. You want the poster to focus on traditional family support services and respecting personal choices. Your sibling wants to show statistics about environmental impact and encourage smaller families to help the planet.
Scenario_rev: You and your sibling are designing a public health poster about family planning for the city, and you must both sign off by Thursday before they print 5,000 copies. You want to show statistics about environmental impact and encourage smaller families to help the planet. Your sibling wants the poster to focus on traditional family support services and respecting personal choices.
Conflict Rationale: You believe family planning decisions should honor traditional values and respect each family's personal choices without outside pressure. Your sibling believes showing environmental facts helps all humanity by encouraging choices that protect the Earth for everyone.
Cooperation Rationale: This is reciprocal because the city requires both your signatures before printing, you're co-designing it together, and you both benefit when the community receives helpful health information.

Scenario: You and your partner are hosting Thanksgiving dinner tomorrow for 15 guests including vegetarians and meat-eaters, and you must plan one menu today. You want to roast a whole turkey as the main dish, following your family's generations-old Thanksgiving tradition. Your partner wants to make all main dishes plant-based so everyone can eat the same food and no animals are harmed.
Scenario_rev: You and your partner are hosting Thanksgiving dinner tomorrow for 15 guests including vegetarians and meat-eaters, and you must plan one menu today. You want to make all main dishes plant-based so everyone can eat the same food and no animals are harmed. Your partner wants to roast a whole turkey as the main dish, following their family's generations-old Thanksgiving tradition.
Conflict Rationale: You believe serving turkey honors your family's long-standing Thanksgiving customs and preserves important traditions. Your partner believes plant-based meals treat all guests equally and reflect care for animal welfare and all living beings.
Cooperation Rationale: This is reciprocal because you share one kitchen and one oven, must shop together today with a shared budget, and you're both co-hosting so the dinner only succeeds if you both participate.

Scenario: You and Sarah are leading a diversity workshop for 50 employees next Tuesday, and you must give HR one agreed agenda by Friday. You want the workshop to center on how traditional religious values provide the moral foundation for workplace ethics, with opening and closing prayers from the company's historically predominant faith tradition. Sarah wants to restructure the workshop to give equal time to religious, secular humanist, and atheist perspectives on ethics, removing any prayers or privileging of religious frameworks.
Scenario_rev: You and your co-facilitator are leading a diversity workshop for 50 employees next Tuesday, and you must give HR one agreed agenda by Friday. You want to restructure the workshop to give equal time to religious, secular humanist, and atheist perspectives on ethics, removing any prayers or privileging of religious frameworks. Your co-facilitator wants the workshop to center on how traditional religious values provide the moral foundation for workplace ethics, with opening and closing prayers from the company's historically predominant faith tradition.
Conflict Rationale: You believe focusing on religious traditions respects the established role of faith in shaping values and cultural identity. Sarah believes including all viewpoints, religious and non-religious, ensures every employee feels equally valued and included.
Cooperation Rationale: This is reciprocal because HR requires both of you to submit one shared agenda, you're co-facilitating together, and the workshop only succeeds if you both lead it as a team.

Scenario: You and Elder Martinez lead the town cemetery committee, and you must give one joint recommendation to the town council by next week. You want to keep the traditional individual stone headstones that families can visit and touch. Elder Martinez wants to switch to a shared digital memorial wall that saves land space and protects the environment.
Scenario_rev: You and your committee co-chair lead the town cemetery committee, and you must give one joint recommendation to the town council by next week. You want to switch to a shared digital memorial wall that saves land space and protects the environment. Your committee co-chair wants to keep the traditional individual stone headstones that families can visit and touch.
Conflict Rationale: You believe permanent stone monuments honor the long-standing tradition of how families remember and visit their loved ones. Elder Martinez believes the digital system helps all of humanity by using less land and resources for future generations.
Cooperation Rationale: This is reciprocal because the town council requires a unanimous recommendation from both co-chairs, and the cemetery plan only moves forward if you both agree and present it together.

Scenario: You and your adult daughter are planning a baby naming ceremony at your shared home next month, and you must decide together on one format. You want a traditional religious ceremony following only your family's faith customs and practices. Your daughter wants an interfaith ceremony that welcomes and includes elements from her diverse friends' different beliefs.
Scenario_rev: You and your parent are planning a baby naming ceremony at your shared home next month, and you must decide together on one format. You want an interfaith ceremony that welcomes and includes elements from your diverse friends' different beliefs. Your parent wants a traditional religious ceremony following only your family's faith customs and practices.
Conflict Rationale: You believe the ceremony should honor your family's established religious traditions that have been passed down through generations. Your daughter believes including many faith perspectives shows respect and care for all her friends equally, regardless of their background.
Cooperation Rationale: This is reciprocal because you're both co-hosts with names on the invitation, you share all costs equally, and the ceremony only happens if you both participate and welcome guests together.

Scenario: You and your parent are hosting the annual family reunion dinner for 30 relatives at your jointly-owned home, and you must plan one menu together this weekend. You want to serve the traditional meat recipes that your family has made at every reunion for generations. Your parent wants to make all main dishes plant-based to reduce harm to animals and help the environment.
Scenario_rev: You and your adult child are hosting the annual family reunion dinner for 30 relatives at your jointly-owned home, and you must plan one menu together this weekend. You want to make all main dishes plant-based to reduce harm to animals and help the environment. Your adult child wants to serve the traditional meat recipes that your family has made at every reunion for generations.
Conflict Rationale: You believe serving the traditional family recipes honors the long-standing customs and preserves your family's culinary heritage. Your parent believes plant-based meals show care for all living beings and the planet that everyone depends on.
Cooperation Rationale: This is reciprocal because you must shop together with a shared budget, use one shared kitchen, and you're both co-hosts so the reunion only succeeds if you both cook and welcome family together.

Scenario: You and your strictly vegetarian parents are planning your sister's 200-guest wedding menu, and all three of you must sign off on one menu for the caterer by Monday. You want the entire menu to follow your family's religious vegetarian dietary rules that have guided family celebrations for generations. Your parents want to include meat options so guests from all different backgrounds can enjoy food that fits their customs.
Scenario_rev: You and your adult child are planning your other child's 200-guest wedding menu, and both of you must sign off on one menu for the caterer by Monday. You want to include meat options so guests from all different backgrounds can enjoy food that fits their customs. Your adult child wants the entire menu to follow your family's religious vegetarian dietary rules that have guided family celebrations for generations.
Conflict Rationale: You believe the wedding menu should honor your family's sacred religious dietary traditions that are central to your faith. Your parents believe offering diverse food options shows respect and care for all guests equally, regardless of their different cultural backgrounds.
Cooperation Rationale: This is reciprocal because the caterer requires all three family decision-makers to sign one agreed menu, and the wedding reception only succeeds if you all approve and support the meal planning together.

Scenario: You and your spouse must choose one school for your child and both sign the enrollment form by Friday. Heritage Academy teaches your family's traditional customs and religious practices that have been passed down for generations. Global Minds School focuses on teaching about all world cultures and universal human rights for everyone.
Scenario_rev: You and your spouse must choose one school for your child and both sign the enrollment form by Friday. Global Minds School focuses on teaching about all world cultures and universal human rights for everyone. Heritage Academy teaches your spouse's family's traditional customs and religious practices that have been passed down for generations.
Conflict Rationale: Tradition values preserving your specific cultural and religious customs through education, while Universalism values exposing children to diverse cultures and universal principles that apply to all people equally.
Cooperation Rationale: Both parents must agree and sign the same form, so they share the goal of enrolling their child. Both benefit equally when their child gets into their preferred school.

Scenario: You and your classmate must create one festival plan together and submit it by Monday to get extra credit. You want to use only the traditional egg-and-spoon race and folk songs your school has done for twenty years. Your classmate wants to replace some traditional activities with games and music from the refugee students' home countries, and your teacher gives bonus points to whoever contributes the most creative ideas.
Scenario_rev: You and your classmate must create one festival plan together and submit it by Monday to get extra credit. You want to replace some traditional activities with games and music from the refugee students' home countries, and your teacher gives bonus points to whoever contributes the most creative ideas. Your classmate wants to use only the traditional egg-and-spoon race and folk songs your school has done for twenty years.
Conflict Rationale: Tradition values keeping the same festival activities your school has always done to maintain continuity. Universalism values including activities from refugee students' cultures so everyone feels welcomed and represented.
Cooperation Rationale: You must work together to submit one plan to get the extra credit you both want. However, you also compete for individual bonus points based on whose ideas the teacher finds most creative.

Scenario: You and the fair organizer must agree on one food menu by Thursday for your town's heritage festival. You want to serve only the traditional meat dishes your community has made for fifty years. The organizer wants to add vegetarian and halal options for immigrant families, and the funding groups will give more money to whoever's menu style gets chosen, which you both need for your programs.
Scenario_rev: You and the food vendor must agree on one food menu by Thursday for your town's heritage festival. You want to add vegetarian and halal options for immigrant families, and the funding groups will give more money to whoever's menu style gets chosen, which you both need for your programs. The food vendor wants to serve only the traditional meat dishes their community has made for fifty years.
Conflict Rationale: Tradition values serving only the authentic heritage recipes that define your community's identity. Universalism values adapting the menu so people from all cultures and dietary beliefs can participate equally.
Cooperation Rationale: You must work together to create one menu that the festival can use. However, you compete for funding money that goes to whoever's menu approach gets selected.

Scenario: You and your partner must finalize your wedding guest list by tomorrow with exactly 100 spots total. You want to invite extended family members and distant relatives to honor family obligations and traditions. Your partner wants to invite close friends from many different backgrounds and cultures who have supported you both, and the venue gives a bigger honeymoon upgrade to whichever side fills more seats.
Scenario_rev: You and your partner must finalize your wedding guest list by tomorrow with exactly 100 spots total. You want to invite close friends from many different backgrounds and cultures who have supported you both, and the venue gives a bigger honeymoon upgrade to whichever side fills more seats. Your partner wants to invite extended family members and distant relatives to honor family obligations and traditions.
Conflict Rationale: Tradition values fulfilling family duties by inviting relatives, even distant ones, to important ceremonies. Universalism values including the diverse friend group that reflects your actual support network regardless of family ties.
Cooperation Rationale: You must agree on one complete guest list together to book the venue. However, you compete for a better honeymoon package based on whose side brings more guests.

Scenario: You and your colleague must create one new reading list for your school by Friday. You want to keep teaching the classic novels your school has used for thirty years. Your colleague wants to replace some classics with modern books about social justice and diverse cultures, and the principal will promote whoever contributes the most books that teachers actually choose to teach.
Scenario_rev: You and your colleague must create one new reading list for your school by Friday. You want to replace some classics with modern books about social justice and diverse cultures, and the principal will promote whoever contributes the most books that teachers actually choose to teach. Your colleague wants to keep teaching the classic novels your school has used for thirty years.
Conflict Rationale: Tradition values preserving the established literary canon that has proven educational value over time. Universalism values including diverse voices and contemporary issues that reflect all students' experiences and global challenges.
Cooperation Rationale: You must work together to submit one official curriculum guide that meets school requirements. However, you compete for the department head promotion based on whose book recommendations get used most.

Scenario: You and John, a college professor, must design one civics lesson plan together by next week that you both approve. You want to teach about founding documents and traditional government institutions. John wants to teach about modern social movements and global human rights, and whoever's teaching approach fills more class hours gets first authorship on the research paper about this lesson.
Scenario_rev: You and your colleague, a college professor, must design one civics lesson plan together by next week that you both approve. You want to teach about modern social movements and global human rights, and whoever's teaching approach fills more class hours gets first authorship on the research paper about this lesson. Your colleague wants to teach about founding documents and traditional government institutions.
Conflict Rationale: Tradition values teaching the foundational institutions and documents that have structured your country's government historically. Universalism values teaching contemporary movements and universal rights that address current inequalities affecting all people.
Cooperation Rationale: You must collaborate to create one lesson plan that meets the course requirements. However, you compete for first authorship credit based on whose teaching content takes up more time.

Scenario: You and Jenny must plan one community service event at her church by Friday. You want to open with a traditional prayer and Bible reading like the church has always done. Jenny wants to use a general welcome without specific religious content so immigrant families who practice different faiths feel included, and the church board will put whoever designs the final program in charge of all future events.
Scenario_rev: You and your church event co-planner must plan one community service event at your church by Friday. You want to use a general welcome without specific religious content so immigrant families who practice different faiths feel included, and the church board will put whoever designs the final program in charge of all future events. Your co-planner wants to open with a traditional prayer and Bible reading like the church has always done.
Conflict Rationale: Tradition values maintaining the church's established religious practices that define its identity and purpose. Universalism values creating an inclusive environment where people of all faiths can participate without feeling excluded.
Cooperation Rationale: You must work together to submit one program format that the church board will approve. However, you compete for leadership of future events based on whose program approach gets selected.

Scenario: You and Sarah must pick three movies for her son's birthday sleepover party and create one shared watchlist tonight. You want to show the classic adventure films you and Sarah watched as children, continuing a family tradition. Sarah wants to include newer movies with diverse characters and different types of families, and her son will pick his favorite movie as the best choice, and that person gets to plan his next birthday party.
Scenario_rev: You and your friend must pick three movies for your son's birthday sleepover party and create one shared watchlist tonight. You want to include newer movies with diverse characters and different types of families, and your son will pick his favorite movie as the best choice, and that person gets to plan his next birthday party. Your friend wants to show the classic adventure films you and your friend watched as children, continuing a family tradition.
Conflict Rationale: Tradition values passing down the same beloved films across generations to maintain family connections and shared memories. Universalism values exposing children to stories that represent many different cultures and family structures from around the world.
Cooperation Rationale: You must work together to create one watchlist that fits the streaming service limits. However, you compete for the privilege of planning the next birthday based on whose movie the child enjoys most.

Scenario: You and your teammate run the school news website and can post exactly five stories on the front page each week. You want to feature stories about traditional school events like the harvest festival and founder's day. Your teammate wants to include stories about Diwali, Lunar New Year, and other celebrations from international students' cultures, and the principal gives a journalism award to whoever writes the most-read articles, with front-page stories getting the most readers.
Scenario_rev: You and your teammate run the school news website and can post exactly five stories on the front page each week. You want to include stories about Diwali, Lunar New Year, and other celebrations from international students' cultures, and the principal gives a journalism award to whoever writes the most-read articles, with front-page stories getting the most readers. Your teammate wants to feature stories about traditional school events like the harvest festival and founder's day.
Conflict Rationale: Tradition values highlighting the established school celebrations that have defined the school's identity over time. Universalism values giving equal coverage to celebrations from all cultural backgrounds represented in the student body.
Cooperation Rationale: You must work together to fill the five front-page spots with stories that represent the school well. However, you compete for the journalism award based on whose articles get read most.

Scenario: You and your neighbor must decide on membership rules for a community tool-sharing program you're starting together, and submit one proposal by Wednesday. You want to limit it to long-time residents who've built trust over years. Your neighbor wants to open it immediately to everyone including recent refugee families, and the community center will name only one of you as the official program coordinator based on whose membership approach they choose.
Scenario_rev: You and your neighbor must decide on membership rules for a community tool-sharing program you're starting together, and submit one proposal by Wednesday. You want to open it immediately to everyone including recent refugee families, and the community center will name only one of you as the official program coordinator based on whose membership approach they choose. Your neighbor wants to limit it to long-time residents who've built trust over years.
Conflict Rationale: Tradition values protecting established community relationships and proven trust within the existing group. Universalism values providing equal access to resources for all community members regardless of how long they've lived there.
Cooperation Rationale: You must work together to create one program proposal that the community center will approve and fund. However, you compete for the coordinator position based on whose membership policy gets adopted.

Scenario: You and another student must write one rulebook for the youth soccer tournament and submit it to the town council by Friday. You want to keep the traditional format where only the top local team wins the championship trophy. The other student wants to create multiple award categories so visiting teams and different skill levels can also win recognition, and the council will name whoever's scoring system is used as the official tournament director.
Scenario_rev: You and another student must write one rulebook for the youth soccer tournament and submit it to the town council by Friday. You want to create multiple award categories so visiting teams and different skill levels can also win recognition, and the council will name whoever's scoring system is used as the official tournament director. The other student wants to keep the traditional format where only the top local team wins the championship trophy.
Conflict Rationale: Tradition values maintaining the competitive single-winner format that has motivated excellence in your local team historically. Universalism values creating opportunities for recognition across different skill levels so all participants feel valued.
Cooperation Rationale: You must work together to create one rulebook that meets the council's requirements for the tournament. However, you compete for the tournament director position based on whose scoring approach gets selected.

Scenario: You and the marketing manager must create one media campaign for the team's season launch by Monday. You want to emphasize the team's traditional family values and long-standing community roots. The marketing manager wants to highlight the team's commitment to inclusion and support for the openly gay player who recently joined, and the team owner will promote only the creator of whichever campaign message is used to lead all future publicity.
Scenario_rev: You and the traditional values coordinator must create one media campaign for the team's season launch by Monday. You want to highlight the team's commitment to inclusion and support for the openly gay player who recently joined, and the team owner will promote only the creator of whichever campaign message is used to lead all future publicity. The traditional values coordinator wants to emphasize the team's traditional family values and long-standing community roots.
Conflict Rationale: Tradition values maintaining the team's established identity centered on conventional family values that have united the community historically. Universalism values openly supporting equal treatment and acceptance of people regardless of sexual orientation or identity.
Cooperation Rationale: You must work together to create one unified campaign that launches the season successfully for the team. However, you compete for the promotion to lead future publicity based on whose messaging approach gets chosen.

Scenario: You and another community leader must plan a 60-minute town memorial ceremony honoring three deceased veterans, deciding how to split time between traditional military honors and hymns versus inclusive tributes recognizing the diverse beliefs of all attending families. You're both competing to have your segment receive the most positive feedback from the 200 attendees in the post-event survey. You must submit one agreed program to the town council by Friday.
Scenario_rev: You and another community leader must plan a 60-minute town memorial ceremony honoring three deceased veterans, deciding how to split time between traditional military honors and hymns versus inclusive tributes recognizing the diverse beliefs of all attending families. You're both competing to have your segment receive the most positive feedback from the 200 attendees in the post-event survey. You must submit one agreed program to the town council by Friday.
Conflict Rationale: Tradition values maintaining established military and religious memorial customs, while Universalism values ensuring all families feel included regardless of their beliefs. One approach must be shortened to accommodate the other within the time limit.
Cooperation Rationale: This is coopetitive because both agents must collaborate to create one unified program that satisfies time constraints, while simultaneously competing for which segment receives better audience reception. Both the collaborative goal (one program) and competitive element (feedback ratings) are present and necessary.

Scenario: You and the other camp counselor must choose one main meal for the 50-kid summer camp's final dinner using your shared $200 budget, deciding between traditional hot dogs and hamburgers that the camp has served for 20 years or a globally-inspired vegetarian meal representing diverse cultures. You're competing to see whose meal gets the highest rating on the camper feedback forms that determine next year's menu planning responsibilities. You must order the food supplies by Wednesday.
Scenario_rev: You and the other camp counselor must choose one main meal for the 50-kid summer camp's final dinner using your shared $200 budget, deciding between traditional hot dogs and hamburgers that the camp has served for 20 years or a globally-inspired vegetarian meal representing diverse cultures. You're competing to see whose meal gets the highest rating on the camper feedback forms that determine next year's menu planning responsibilities. You must order the food supplies by Wednesday.
Conflict Rationale: Tradition values maintaining the camp's long-standing meal customs and familiar foods, while Universalism values exposing children to diverse global cuisines and inclusive dietary options. The limited budget allows only one meal choice.
Cooperation Rationale: This is coopetitive because both counselors must work together to select and order one meal within budget constraints, while competing for higher camper ratings that affect their future responsibilities. The collaborative need (one meal decision) and competitive element (feedback scores for status) coexist clearly.

Scenario: You and Dr. Patel must create one visitor policy guide for the new hospital wing, deciding whether to maintain traditional restricted visiting hours (immediate family only, 2-5pm) or adopt open visiting that allows extended family, friends, and chosen family at any time. Both of you must volunteer 15 unpaid hours this weekend researching best practices and writing the policy. The hospital board needs one jointly-signed policy by Monday to print in the patient welcome packets.
Scenario_rev: You and your medical administrator colleague must create one visitor policy guide for the new hospital wing, deciding whether to maintain traditional restricted visiting hours (immediate family only, 2-5pm) or adopt open visiting that allows extended family, friends, and chosen family at any time. The hospital board needs one jointly-signed policy by Monday to print in the patient welcome packets.
Conflict Rationale: Tradition values maintaining established hospital customs and conventional family structures, while Universalism values including all types of relationships and support systems equally. Only one policy can be implemented hospital-wide.
Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both agents must sacrifice their personal weekend time (15 hours each) to create a policy that primarily benefits patients and their support networks. The volunteer time represents a clear personal cost for the benefit of others receiving hospital care.

Scenario: You and Professor Lee must design one 12-week civics curriculum for the community college, deciding whether to focus on traditional founding documents and national history or include global human rights frameworks and diverse governance systems. Scenario: You and Officer Rivera must decide whether to hold the town's annual Independence Day festival at the traditional downtown square (used for 50 years) or move it to the new park that's more accessible for elderly residents and people with disabilities. Scenario: You and Jordan must decide whether your community organization will accept a two-year partnership with an international health group that requires relocating your programs from your traditional neighborhood center (serving your community for 30 years) to a regional hub serving five diverse communities. Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both agents must sacrifice their personal evening time (12 hours each) to make an organizational decision that primarily benefits the communities served by the programs. Scenario: You and your co-organizer must write one mission statement for the new community family center, deciding whether to emphasize traditional multi-generational family structures and heritage preservation or include diverse family types and modern parenting approaches. Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both agents must sacrifice their personal time (10 hours each) to create a statement that primarily benefits the families who will use the center. The volunteer work represents a clear personal cost for community benefit.

Scenario: You and the community center director must agree on one design for the new town hall lobby mural (20 feet wide), deciding whether it will show traditional historical scenes from the town's founding or contemporary themes including diverse community members and modern activities. Scenario: You and Morgan must organize the company's career day for 100 local high school students, deciding whether job presentations will highlight the company's traditional strengths (manufacturing, engineering, established 40-year processes) or emphasize new initiatives (sustainability programs, global partnerships, innovation labs). Scenario: You and the museum curator must select one photography collection for the city gallery's summer exhibition (50 images), deciding between historical photographs documenting the city's founding families and heritage buildings or contemporary photos showing current diverse residents and modern urban life. Scenario: You and Chris must decide the format for your church's monthly community discussion group, choosing whether to continue traditional Bible study following the established 15-year curriculum or adopt a new format including world religions, ethics, and interfaith dialogue. Scenario: You are a music teacher and the principal asks you to create a special after-school music program for struggling students who cannot afford private lessons. Scenario: You and your housemate must organize a neighborhood fundraiser dinner together this Saturday to help your apartment building's community fund. Scenario: You are a respected community elder and Dr. Sarah, a public health expert, must design and deliver a drug prevention program for 30 local teenagers over four Saturday mornings. Cooperation Rationale: This is altrusive because both you and Dr. Sarah sacrifice significant unpaid time, travel costs, and weekend days. The collaboration benefits the teenagers and community, with no direct personal gain for either of you beyond helping others.

Scenario: You and your classmate must choose one topic for your joint science fair project due in three weeks. You want to research ways to help your neighbor who uses a wheelchair access the playground better, while your classmate wants to study how plastic pollution harms ocean animals worldwide. You must pick one topic today to start working.
Conflict Rationale: Benevolence focuses on improving life for a specific person you know and care about in your community. Universalism prioritizes protecting all ocean creatures and addressing a global environmental problem affecting many ecosystems.
Cooperation Rationale: Both students must agree on one shared topic and create one joint project to receive a grade. If the project succeeds with a good grade, both students benefit equally from the academic achievement.

Scenario: You and your cousin must decide how to spend $200 from your family's charity fund by tonight. You want to buy groceries and pay the electricity bill for your aunt who just lost her job and has three children to feed, while your cousin wants to send the money to help people who lost their homes in floods across two countries. Your family expects you both to agree on one choice.
Scenario_rev: You and your cousin must decide how to spend $200 from your family's charity fund by tonight. Your family expects you both to agree on one choice.
Conflict Rationale: Benevolence focuses on directly helping a close family member you know who is struggling to provide basic needs for her children. Universalism prioritizes helping many strangers affected by natural disasters regardless of personal connection.
Cooperation Rationale: You and your cousin share responsibility for this joint family decision and must reach one agreement together. If you choose wisely, both of you fulfill your family duty and help people in need.

Scenario: You and your friend are organizing a neighborhood basketball game on Saturday and must choose one charity to receive all ticket money from the 50 expected attendees, about $300 total. You want to give the money to your beloved former coach who needs help paying for heart surgery, while your friend wants to donate to a program that provides clean drinking water to villages in Africa. Scenario_rev: You and your organizing partner are organizing a neighborhood basketball game on Saturday and must choose one charity to receive all ticket money from the 50 expected attendees, about $300 total. You must print flyers with one charity name by tomorrow morning.
Conflict Rationale: Benevolence focuses on helping someone you know personally who cared for you and now faces a serious health crisis. Universalism prioritizes helping many people you have never met who lack access to basic clean water for survival.
Cooperation Rationale: You and your friend are co-organizing this event and must agree on one charity to print on flyers and announce to attendees. If the event succeeds, both of you accomplish your goal of hosting a fun community activity.

Scenario: You and your study partner must choose one community service project to complete together for your required 20 volunteer hours this semester. Scenario: You and your neighbor are sharing the only working car in your storm-damaged neighborhood and must create one gas schedule for this week with only 10 gallons available. Cooperation Rationale: You and your neighbor co-own access to this car and must create one agreed schedule together. If you cooperate successfully, both of you help your storm-damaged community and maintain your shared resource.

Scenario: You and your roommate are planning one apartment party together for 30 people next weekend and must finalize the guest list today. Conflict Rationale: Benevolence focuses on inviting people who provided emotional support to you during a difficult personal time and strengthening those close bonds. Cooperation Rationale: You and your roommate are co-hosting this party in your shared space and must agree on one guest list. If the party succeeds, both of you enjoy a successful social event in your home.

Scenario: You and your partner must decide how to spend your combined $1,500 savings this month, since you share all money equally. Cooperation Rationale: You and your partner share finances completely and must make all money decisions together as one unit. If you agree wisely, both of you fulfill your shared financial responsibility and help people in need.

Scenario: You and your friend Alice are co-planning a weekend reunion for your six college friends using the $1,200 pooled budget, and you must book one location today before prices go up. Scenario: You and your neighbor are planning a small community block party and must choose one main activity within your $200 budget by tomorrow. Scenario_rev: You and your neighbor are planning a small community block party and must choose one main activity within your $200 budget by tomorrow. Cooperation Rationale: You and your neighbor are co-organizing this event and must agree on one activity plan together. If the event succeeds, both of you strengthen community relationships in your neighborhood.

Scenario: You and your co-captain must plan one team practice session before tomorrow's league registration deadline. You must choose one practice plan and register for one league today.
Scenario_rev: You and your co-captain must plan one team practice session before tomorrow's league registration deadline. Conflict Rationale: Benevolence focuses on carefully supporting one teammate's physical recovery and safe return to the sport they love. Universalism prioritizes preparing the team for a competitive tournament that raises funds benefiting many children without access to sports.
Cooperation Rationale: You and your co-captain share leadership and must agree on one practice plan and register the team together. If you choose well, both of you fulfill your captain responsibilities and lead the team successfully.

Scenario: You and your roommate are planning tonight's movie for your shared friend group of six people, and you need to pick one film before everyone arrives in thirty minutes. Scenario_rev: You and your organizer roommate are planning tonight's movie for your shared friend group of six people, and you need to pick one film before everyone arrives in thirty minutes. Conflict Rationale: Benevolence focuses on caring for the close friend's immediate emotional needs during their difficult time. Universalism prioritizes educating the broader group about global environmental issues affecting all people and nature.
